name: Civil Service Principles and Ethics
description: Core principles, values, ethics, and code of conduct for civil servants.

subcategories.name: Civil Service Principles & Ethics
subcategories.description: Core values, ethics, and public service principles.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the Civil Service Handbook
options: To regulate financial management procedures.
To provide ethical and operational guidance for civil servants.
To manage the retirement funds of public officers.
To define promotion quotas for MDAs.
questions.explanation: The primary purpose of the Civil Service Handbook is to provide ethical and operational guidance to civil servants to ensure effective service delivery.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to the Civil Service Handbook, which three values must civil servants uphold
options: Partisanship, favoritism, and delay.
Secrecy, efficiency, and loyalty.
Apathy, secrecy, and dishonesty.
Nepotism, inefficiency, and delay.
questions.explanation: The handbook emphasizes secrecy, efficiency, and loyalty as core values of civil servants (Implied by core values outlined in CSH sections).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The Code of Conduct for public officers is primarily outlined in which part of the Civil Service Handbook
options: Part I.
Chapter 6.
Chapter 10.
Part IV.
questions.explanation: Part I of the Civil Service Handbook outlines the Code of Conduct for public officers (PSR 100401).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which scenario constitutes a **Conflict of Interest** that civil servants must avoid
options: Attending a mandatory training course.
Receiving the monthly salary payment.
Owning shares in a company that competes for government contracts.
Taking approved annual leave.
questions.explanation: Civil servants must avoid conflicts of interest, such as owning shares in companies competing for government contracts.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The primary duty of civil servants concerning service delivery is emphasized as:
options: Promoting personal business interests.
Providing impartial and efficient service.
Disclosing government secrets to the public.
Avoiding all forms of accountability.
questions.explanation: The duty of civil servants is to provide impartial, efficient, and accountable service to the public.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of political neutrality requires civil servants to:
options: Openly campaign for the ruling party.
Serve all administrations equally, regardless of political party.
Reject all orders originating from political appointees.
Refrain from voting in national elections.
questions.explanation: Political neutrality requires civil servants to serve any government of the day equally, without political bias.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which activity is explicitly **NOT** considered a duty of civil servants under the Handbook
options: Loyalty to the government.
Protection of public funds.
Engagement in partisan politics.
Promotion of efficiency.
questions.explanation: Civil servants are prohibited from engaging in partisan politics to maintain neutrality and integrity (PSR 100402(f)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of anonymity guiding civil servants means they should:
options: Avoid disclosing any personal details to the government.
Serve without seeking personal recognition for government policies.
Be prohibited from signing official documents.
Avoid all interactions with the media.
questions.explanation: Anonymity means civil servants serve the government without seeking personal recognition for policies implemented.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Civil servants are prohibited from accepting gifts from the public to mitigate the risk of:
options: Reduced professional development.
Conflict of interest and corruption.
Breach of accountability.
Reduced workload.
questions.explanation: Accepting gifts could lead to conflicts of interest and corruption, which is prohibited.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which qualities are emphasized as essential for effective leadership in the civil service
options: Secrecy and favoritism.
Integrity and accountability.
Nepotism and bias.
Incompetence and laziness.
questions.explanation: Integrity and accountability are foundational principles (PSR Foreword).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Why is impartiality vital in the civil service
options: To ensure personal interests are prioritized.
To guarantee fair and unbiased service to all citizens.
To favor the political agenda of the ruling party.
To reduce workload and administrative effort.
questions.explanation: Impartiality guarantees that civil servants serve all citizens fairly without political or personal bias.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle that ensures civil servants are non-partisan in their duties is:
options: Accountability.
Neutrality.
Permanence.
Transparency.
questions.explanation: Neutrality ensures civil servants perform their duties without political bias.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What core principle ensures civil servants remain answerable for their official actions and decisions
options: Anonymity.
Neutrality.
Accountability.
Efficiency.
questions.explanation: Accountability ensures civil servants remain answerable for their actions and decisions.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The ethical foundation of public service, as emphasized in the **PSR**, is based on:
options: Political affiliation and loyalty.
Personal relationships and tribalism.
Integrity and accountability.
Seniority and length of service.
questions.explanation: The PSR promotes Transparency, Rule of Law, and Accountability as foundational principles.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which constitutional instrument provides the ethical standards, including the mandate for asset declaration, for public officers
options: The Public Procurement Act, 2007.
The Fifth Schedule (Code of Conduct) of the Constitution.
The Financial Regulations.
The Freedom of Information Act.
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct for public officers is contained in the Fifth Schedule of the 1999 Constitution (Implied constitutional mandate).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The primary purpose of the mandatory asset declaration requirement for public officers is to prevent:
options: Failure to pay taxes.
Conflicts of interest and illicit enrichment.
Excessive annual leave.
Disengagement from service.
questions.explanation: Asset declaration (Code of Conduct provisions) is mandated to prevent conflicts of interest and monitor financial integrity.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which civil service value contradicts the principles of professionalism and accountability
options: Impartiality.
Neutrality.
Integrity.
Impunity.
questions.explanation: Impunity (exemption from punishment) directly contradicts civil service principles of accountability and the rule of law.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Permanence** in the civil service ensures what outcome
options: Continuous employment until age 65.
Strict adherence to political bias.
Continuity of policies despite changes in political administration.
Permanent transfer between ministries.
questions.explanation: Permanence ensures that the civil service continues its functions despite political changes, providing stability and institutional memory.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Upon appointment, every officer is statutorily required to sign which document in line with **PSR** 020210
options: Oath of Secrecy (or Security).
Certificate of Service.
Virement Warrant.
Performance Appraisal Form.
questions.explanation: Rule 020210 makes it the duty of the Permanent Secretary to ensure newly employed staff sign an Oath of Secrecy.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Contravention of **PSR** **Rule 020211** (Prohibition of Membership of Secret Society) is regarded as an act of:
options: Minor Misconduct.
General Inefficiency.
Serious Misconduct.
Political Activity.
questions.explanation: The prohibition against secret societies is a serious conduct issue (PSR contextually similar to Serious Misconduct leading to dismissal).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Civil servants must treat all official documents as the property of whom
options: The Head of Department.
The Government.
Themselves.
The Public (under FOI).
questions.explanation: All official documents are government property, not the personal property of officers.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The fundamental duty of civil servants concerning policy implementation is to serve:
options: Political leaders and private companies.
The public impartially and effectively.
Foreign governments and international partners.
Only the highest-ranking officer.
questions.explanation: The ultimate duty of civil servants is to serve the public impartially and effectively.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to the Civil Service Handbook, public resources must be used:
options: For personal gain if the officer is senior.
Responsibly and strictly for official purposes.
Shared with family members.
Diverted to private development projects.
questions.explanation: Public resources must be used responsibly and strictly for official purposes (Implied by accountability principles).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The prohibition of Nepotism in civil service appointments means avoiding:
options: Favoring family and friends in appointments.
Giving priority to qualified candidates.
Following due process in recruitment.
Refusing to fill advertised vacancies.
questions.explanation: Nepotism refers to giving undue advantage to family or friends in recruitment and appointments.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The requirement for civil servants to maintain secrecy in official matters is enforced by which legislative instrument
options: The Public Procurement Act.
The Official Secrets Act.
The Freedom of Information Act.
The Appropriation Act.
questions.explanation: The Official Secrets Act (Cap. 335) enforces secrecy requirements for civil servants handling official matters,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to the **PSR**, engaging in partisan political activities is classified as an act of:
options: General Inefficiency.
Minor Misconduct.
Serious Misconduct.
Grievance.
questions.explanation: Engaging in partisan political activities is listed as an act of Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(f), implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The ethical requirement for civil servants to maintain high ethical standards is highlighted in which **PSR** chapter
options: Chapter 4 (Emoluments).
Chapter 10 (Discipline).
Chapter 12 (Leave).
Chapter 15 (Innovations and Inventions).
questions.explanation: Chapter 10 of the PSR covers Discipline, Conduct, and Ethics.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Why is **Efficiency** stressed as a core civil service value
options: To reduce external scrutiny of service outcomes and timelines.
To ensure government services meet public needs effectively.
To prioritize patronage over measurable institutional results.
To separate service delivery from accountability obligations.
questions.explanation: Efficiency ensures civil servants meet public needs effectively and deliver value for money.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Meritocracy** in recruitment and promotion is emphasized in which part of the Civil Service Handbook/**PSR**'
options: Chapter 1 on introductory provisions and interpretation.
Chapter 2 (PSR Rule 020802(d)).
Chapter 5 on leave, transport, and welfare benefits.
Chapter 7 on financial control and accounting procedures.
questions.explanation: Chapter 2 emphasizes merit and competence in recruitment and promotions. Promotion eligibility is based on merit and performance.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The rule against civil servants seeking to influence prominent persons or other individuals to gain appointment, transfer, or promotion is aimed at preventing:
options: Bribery and corruption.
Merit-based promotion.
Compliance with PSR.
Training opportunities.
questions.explanation: PSR 100427 prohibits seeking influence of prominent persons to obtain favors, ensuring decisions are based on merit.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 100402(r**) classifies 'Holding more than one full-time paid job' as:
options: Minor Misconduct.
Serious Misconduct.
General Inefficiency.
Acceptable practice.
questions.explanation: Holding more than one full-time paid job is defined as Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(r), implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The prohibition on receiving or granting preferential treatment (e.g., Nepotism) is classified under:
options: Misconduct.
Serious Misconduct.
General Inefficiency.
Promotion.
questions.explanation: Nepotism or any other form of preferential treatment (PSR 100402(t), implied) is Serious Misconduct.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Civil servants must maintain honesty, integrity, and transparency, as required by:
options: The Appropriation Act.
The Code of Conduct.
The National Assembly Standing Orders.
The Petroleum Act.
questions.explanation: Integrity, honesty, and transparency are emphasized in the Code of Conduct (PSR Foreword).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: If an officer is in doubt about the propriety of accepting a gift, they should consult:
options: Their family lawyer.
The Head of Mission or the Permanent Secretary.
The Auditor-General of the Federation.
The Minister of Justice.
questions.explanation: If an officer is in doubt about the propriety of receiving or retaining a gift, he should consult his Head of Mission or the Permanent Secretary (Foreign Service Regulation No. 8).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which principle guarantees that decisions and processes are open, with records available for accountability
options: Secrecy.
Transparency.
Anonymity.
Neutrality.
questions.explanation: Transparency requires that decisions and processes are open, with records available for accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The act of using government information for private business or profit, which violates ethical standards, is covered by the prohibition against:
options: Nepotism.
Conflict of Interest.
Abnormally Low Bidding.
Misuse of leave.
questions.explanation: Misuse of official information or engaging in activities where personal interests clash with public duties is strictly prohibited under the Conflict of Interest rule.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** promotes the principle of **accountability** through which primary mechanism in staff performance management
options: Annual leave entitlement.
The Annual Performance Evaluation Report (APER/PAF).
Sabbatical leave provision.
Junior Staff Committee meetings.
questions.explanation: The APER/PAF serves as the primary tool to track, assess, and report on individual performance and accountability (PSR 050402).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Why is **Loyalty** considered a core value in the civil service
options: To encourage disobedience.
To ensure dedication to the government and its official policies.
To promote ethnic bias.
To facilitate nepotism.
questions.explanation: Loyalty is essential for ensuring dedication to government policies and programs (Implied core value).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which type of officer is exempted from the general prohibition on engaging in private practice, subject to certain conditions
options: Administrative Officers.
Medical Practitioners and Law Lecturers.
Chief Security Officers.
All Directorate Cadre Officers.
questions.explanation: PSR 100425 provides exemptions for certain professionals like Medical Practitioners and Law Lecturers to engage in private practice.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** emphasizes transparency, rule of law, and which other key principle foundational to achieving an incorruptible public service
options: Partisanship.
Accountability.
Regionalism.
Secrecy.
questions.explanation: The PSR promotes Transparency, Rule of Law, and Accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The duty to avoid **partisan politics** is listed under **PSR** Chapter 10, and violation can lead to:
options: A written warning.
Interdiction.
Dismissal.
A salary bonus.
questions.explanation: Engaging in partisan political activities is Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(f), implied), which may lead to dismissal.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 100409** mandates that an officer charged with a criminal offense must promptly report the charge and its outcome to whom
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts in the Ministry.
His Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Chair of the Ministerial Disciplinary Committee.
questions.explanation: An Officer must, under pain of disciplinary action, promptly report to his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office whenever he is charged with a criminal offense (PSR 100409, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The use of the principle of **Anonymity** ensures that praise or blame for government policies falls upon:
options: The Civil Servant.
The Political Appointee (Minister/President).
The media.
The Head of Civil Service.
questions.explanation: Anonymity means civil servants serve the government without seeking personal recognition for policies implemented.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Civil servants are discouraged from joining or maintaining membership in any **Secret Society** under which **PSR** Rule
questions.explanation: PSR 020211 explicitly prohibits public officers from being members of any secret society (Implied rule reference).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The core ethical value that emphasizes professional competency, dedication, and following all lawful instructions is:
options: Secrecy.
Efficiency.
Loyalty.
Anonymity.
questions.explanation: Loyalty implies dedication and commitment to the execution of duties and instructions.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The primary ethical requirement ensuring that officers dedicate their entire attention to the Service, except where specific permission is granted, is related to:
options: Full time during official hours.
Sick leave application.
Promotion assessment.
Emolument payment.
questions.explanation: An officer is expected to devote full time during official hours to official duties. This underpins the PSR rule against engaging in multiple full-time jobs.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Fifth Schedule** of the Constitution mandates public officers to declare assets:
options: Only upon retirement.
Upon assumption of duty and every four years thereafter.
Annually during the budget cycle.
Only if they are GL 16 and above.
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct requires public officers to declare their assets periodically (upon assumption of duty and every four years, implied constitutional rule).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which principle in the civil service demands that official advice provided to Ministers must be objective and free from personal or political bias
options: Partisanship.
Impartiality.
Delegation.
Permanence.
questions.explanation: Impartial advice ensures ministers make informed decisions based on objective analysis, upholding the principle of Impartiality.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The final authority on matters of ethical conduct and compliance with the **PSR** rests with which body
options: The Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB).
The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The Office of the Head of Civil Service (OHCSF).
The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC).
questions.explanation: The FCSC has ultimate authority on matters of ethical conduct and appeals regarding discipline.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective civil service principles & ethics, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure civil service principles & ethics compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on civil service principles & ethics risk management within Civil Service Principles & Ethics
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
keywords: civil service principles & ethics risk management
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling civil service principles & ethics, which choice reflects proper administrative ethics standards
options: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain grievance handling in Civil Service Principles & Ethics
options: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Civil Service Principles & Ethics, which action best demonstrates performance management
options: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective civil service principles & ethics, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure discipline and conduct
options: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on anti-corruption safeguards within Civil Service Principles & Ethics
options: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling civil service principles & ethics, which choice reflects proper documented procedure standards
options: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Civil Service Principles & Ethics
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

options: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.

questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

options: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.

Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.

questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.

options: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.

options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.

options: Maintain accurate files and update status at each control point.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Maintain accurate files and update status at each control point. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in civil service principles & ethics Reference: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set.

Close cases without validating facts or required records.

questions.question: When handling civil service principles & ethics, which choice reflects proper performance management standards

Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.

options: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.

Apply approved procedures consistently and document each material step.
Apply discretionary shortcuts to accelerate closure regardless of controls.
Bypass review checkpoints where timelines are tight.

Proceed without validating source records and decision criteria.
Delay escalation until issues become material and difficult to reverse.
Treat exceptions as normal practice without written justification.

Bypass review checkpoints where timelines are tight.
Use inconsistent criteria across similar cases in the same period.
Prioritize convenience over approved process requirements.

questions.question: In the public service context, which option best describes the duty and duty of the Executive Council of the Federation?
options: To manage the daily affairs of the Judiciary. □
To elect Local Government Chairmen.
To determine Government policies on various matters.
To confirm appointments of Ministers and Ambassadors.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To determine Government policies on various matters.). The Executive Council of the Federation has the duty and duty of determining Government policies on various matters.

questions.question: which option best describes prohibited under the Principle of Integrity?
options: Exercising leadership.
Being polite to the public. □.
Nepotism, corruption, influence peddling, and illicit enrichment39.
Working hard.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Nepotism, corruption, influence peddling, and illicit enrichment39.). It prohibits the use of public office for personal gain, nepotism, corruption, influence peddling and illicit enrichment. Section D Part 1: Service Delivery

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: The accountability for the conduct and supervision of these examinations for police and paramilitary services lies with whom?
options: The Permanent Secretary of their ministry □
The Federal Civil Service Commission
The respective Service Commissions 52
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The respective Service Commissions 52). The rule specifies that these examinations are "prescribed by their respective Service Commissions," implying that the commissions also conduct and supervise them.

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the principal accountability of the Executive arm of Government?
options: Law making and confirmation of appointments.
Adjudication of disputes.
Monitoring revenue accruals only. □
Day-to-day management of Government, including policy formulation and execution.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Day-to-day management of Government, including policy formulation and execution.). The Executive is in charge for the day-to-day management of Government, and conceives, formulates, executes and monitors policies, programmes and projects.

questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines the main accountability of the Executive arm of government?
options: Day-to-day management of Government.
Adjudicating legal disputes.
Confirming appointments.3.
Law making.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Day-to-day management of Government.). The Executive is accountable for the day-to-day management of Government, including conceiving, formulating, executing, and monitoring policies.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: What does "E-Governance" refer to in the context of service delivery?
options: A method for organizing government meetings. Q.
The application of appropriate information and communication technologies.
A new form of government. 446
A system of political appointments.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The application of appropriate information and communication technologies.). The handbook mentions that physical proximity and accessibility of services can be achieved by the application of appropriate information and communication technologies (E-Governance.

questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes the main duty of all employees of the Federal Government with regard to union activities?
options: To guarantee the union's executives are posted to suitable locations.
To report to the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation. □
To discharge the duties assigned by their employer to the Nigerian public in a timely and efficient manner.
To participate in all union meetings.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To discharge the duties assigned by their employer to the Nigerian public in a timely and efficient manner.). Rule 020609 states that the principal accountability of all employees is "to discharge the duties assigned by their employer to the Nigerian public in a timely and efficient manner."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, select the statement that correctly defines the duty of the Sub-Accounting Officer who issued the imprest when it is retired at another station?
options: To verify the receipt voucher particulars.
To inform the Minister of Finance.
To issue a new imprest.
To conduct an audit. 229 Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To verify the receipt voucher particulars.). Financial Regulation 1011(ii) clarifies: "It is the duty of the Sub-Accounting Officer who issued the cash advance to verify the receipt voucher particulars."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines a key accountability of cash advance holders regarding the use of funds?
options: They are accountable for the correctness of vouchers signed by them.
They are only accountable for cash, not vouchers.a.
They can use other public funds for cash advance purposes if needed.
They can delegate their responsibilities without supervision.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (They are accountable for the correctness of vouchers signed by them.). Financial Regulation 1008 states: "imprest holders must duly observe all regulations regarding the control of expenditure and the disbursement of public money and are accountable for the correctness of vouchers signed by them (see Regulation 410). They are also accountable for ensuring the early submission of paid vouchers for reimbursement. The imprest may be used only for the role for which it is issued and imprest holders must not use other public funds for imprest purposes."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option correctly addresses: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the duty of the relieving officer during a handover of keys and contents?
options: To report any discrepancies later.
To verify the contents and sign the certificate of handing-over.
To discard old records.3.
To accept without verification.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To verify the contents and sign the certificate of handing-over.). (To verify the contents and sign the certificate of handing-over.). Financial Regulation 1125 states, "The relieving officer must satisfy himself that the contents are correct, and will sign the certificate of handing-over." 238

questions.question: In the public service context, which role primarily bears the duty for any discrepancies in financial records within a self-accounting unit?
options: The Accounting Officer.
The junior staff.
The Accountant-General.
The external auditor
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accounting Officer.). The Accounting Officer is charged with ensuring strict observance of regulations and proper accounting, implying ultimate accountability for discrepancies.

questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines the chief objective of government vehicles, as stated in Financial Regulation 2001?
options: For official duties of the government.
For personal use by government officials.
For recreational activities.3
For commercial transportation.

questions.question: choose the best answer for: What defines "official duties" in relation to government vehicle use?
options: Activities directly related to the statutory functions and responsibilities of the government agency. 294
Any activity requested by a government official.
Transportation for family members of government officials. Q
Personal errands if the driver is on duty.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Activities directly related to the statutory functions and responsibilities of the government agency. 294). While not explicitly defined in a single regulation, the overall context of government operations and financial regulations implies that "official duties" are those strictly aligned with the agency's legitimate functions. Part 5: Financial Regulations: Stores Accounting and Custody

options: Report them through the correct channel.
Ignore them, as it is not their duty.
Wait for a superior officer to detect it. Q.
Report them to a friend or colleague.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Report them through the correct channel.). As a good citizen and a good civil servant, one should discourage financial abuses by making suitable reports through the correct channel.

questions.question: In official practice, select the statement that correctly defines the consequence of a false statement in a public officer's declaration of assets?
options: They will be fined a small amount.a.
Nothing, as it is a minor error.
They will be given a warning.
It must be deemed to be a breach of the Code of Conduct.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (It must be deemed to be a breach of the Code of Conduct.). Any statement in such declaration that is found to be false... must be deemed to be a breach of this Code.

questions.question: In the public service context, which option correctly addresses: The handbook lists a number of attributes that Civil Servants should possess to secure quality service delivery. Which of the following is NOT one of them?
options: Be efficient and loyal. □.
Be willing to accept bribes.
Be punctual and dedicated.
Be polite and cheerful.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Be willing to accept bribes.). The handbook lists a number of attributes for Civil Servants to secure quality service delivery, including being punctual, polite, cheerful, efficient, and loyal. Accepting bribes is a corrupt act that is discouraged..

questions.question: select the statement that correctly defines the accountability of the administration regarding participation, consultation, and mediation?
options: To ignore public opinion.
To prevent civil society from participating.
To secure that mechanisms of participation and consultation are effectively put in place.
To only consult with government officials.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To secure that mechanisms of participation and consultation are effectively put in place.). It is the accountability of the administration to guarantee that the mechanisms of participation and consultation involving civil society and other stakeholders are effectively put in place through consultative forums or advisory bodies.

questions.question: select the option that best answers: If the chairman and other members cannot agree on the objective of a meeting, what is the chairman's accountability?
options: To move on with the meeting anyway.
To dismiss the meeting entirely.
To revert to the convening authority to seek clarification.
To force his own interpretation of the role on the members. Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To revert to the convening authority to seek clarification.). Where the chairman and the other members cannot achieve such an understanding or agreement on what the role is, it is the chairman's duty to revert to the convening authority to seek and secure clarification.

options: To guarantee that the minutes are written and circulated, and that all follow-up actions are completed88.
To forget about the meeting and move on. 476
To blame others for the failure of the meeting.3.
To take a long vacation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To guarantee that the minutes are written and circulated, and that all follow-up actions are completed88.). It is the secretary's duty to guarantee that the minutes are written and circulated, and that all follow-up actions are completed.

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: What does "Schemes of Service" refer to?
options: A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.
Disciplinary procedures
Training programs for new recruits.
Retirement benefit plans.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.). Rule 010106 defines "Schemes of Service" as "a reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements, and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of a key accountability of the Accountant-General of the Federation regarding accounting systems and controls?
options: To formulate national economic policy. Q
To personally manage all financial transactions.
To audit all public accounts annually.
To provide adequate accounting systems and controls in all government arms.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To provide adequate accounting systems and controls in all government arms.). Financial Regulation 106 states the Accountant-General "has the accountability for providing adequate accounting systems and controls in the ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and other arms of Government."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: If an officer delegates or entrusts the performance of duties to a subordinate officer, is the delegating officer relieved of accountability?
options: Yes, completely.
Only if the delegation is in writing. □
No, never.
Yes, partially.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (No, never.). Financial Regulation 125 states, "No officer must be relieved of his accountability should he delegate or entrust the performance of duties or any part thereof to his subordinate officer."

questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option best describes the rule concerning the collection of revenue by a Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: They are only in charge for revenue received in cash.
They can delegate this duty to a junior staff member.
They are accountable for departmental control and collection.
They are not involved in revenue collection. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They are accountable for departmental control and collection.). Financial Regulation 217 implies a departmental control over revenue collection, and the Sub-Accounting Officer plays a key role in this.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: What happens to arrears of revenue still outstanding from previous years?
options: They are reported to the Minister of Finance.
They are the accountability of the previous Accounting Officer. Q 167
They are automatically written off.
They must be included in the current year's arrears of revenue returns.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They must be included in the current year's arrears of revenue returns.). Financial Regulation 230(iii) states that arrears of revenue returns must include all arrears still outstanding from previous years if not written off.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: at what point is a Revenue Collector not relieved of their duty by delegation?
options: According to Financial Regulation 125, the delegation of duty does not relieve the officer of their pecuniary responsibilities.
Never, they are always accountable.
Only if the delegation is in writing.
When the person delegated to is also a Revenue Collector
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (According to Financial Regulation 125, the delegation of duty does not relieve the officer of their pecuniary responsibilities.). Financial Regulation 125 states that a delegation of duty to a subordinate officer does not relieve the delegating officer of their pecuniary duty, which applies to Revenue Collectors.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Principles & Ethics - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body is constitutionally empowered to oversee recruitment, promotions, and discipline for the Federal Civil Service
options: Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
National Assembly.
Ministry of Finance.
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC) oversees recruitment, promotions, and discipline of civil servants.

questions.question: The ultimate duty of civil servants is to prioritize service to whom
options: Political party executives.
The public impartially and effectively.
Private consulting companies.
The National Assembly Speaker.
questions.explanation: The ultimate duty of civil servants is to serve the public impartially and effectively.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The Permanent Secretary of a Ministry is statutorily designated as the:
options: Chief Accountant.
Accounting Officer.
Chief Internal Auditor.
Head of the Procurement Unit.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary serves as the accounting officer of the Ministry and ensures accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is explicitly **NOT** a duty of civil servants under the Handbook
options: Loyalty to government.
Protection of public funds.
Engagement in partisan politics.
Promotion of efficiency.
questions.explanation: Civil servants are prohibited from partisan politics to maintain neutrality and integrity.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The primary role of the Civil Service Handbook is to provide what type of guidance for civil servants
options: Financial budgeting strategies.
Ethical and operational guidance.
Criminal prosecution procedures.
International trade laws.
questions.explanation: The Civil Service Handbook provides ethical and operational guidance to civil servants to ensure effective service delivery.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Impartiality is vital in the civil service to guarantee:
options: Favoritism for senior officers.
Fair and unbiased service to all citizens.
Political alignment with the ruling party.
Maximum secrecy in all operations.
questions.explanation: Impartiality is crucial to guarantee fair and unbiased service to all citizens.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Why does the Handbook stress providing objective advice to Ministers
options: To promote personal gain for the officer.
To allow Ministers to make policy decisions objectively.
To encourage regional bias in decision-making.
To reduce the Minister's accountability.
questions.explanation: Impartial advice is necessary to guide policy decisions objectively, ensuring government efficacy.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which principle does the Civil Service Handbook/**PSR** emphasize in recruitment and promotion
options: Regional balancing.
Seniority only.
Nepotism.
Meritocracy.
questions.explanation: Meritocracy is emphasized in recruitment, appointment, and promotion.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which chapter emphasizes service delivery as the core of civil service responsibilities
options: Chapter 1.
Chapter 4.
Chapter 6.
Chapter 7.
questions.explanation: Chapter 4 emphasizes service delivery as the key role of civil servants.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary role of the Registry Unit in an **MDA**'
options: Processing payroll and entitlements.
Managing file movement, correspondence handling, and documentation tracking.
Overseeing procurement activities.
Conducting internal disciplinary inquiries.
questions.explanation: The Registry Unit is responsible for the file movement, correspondence handling, and documentation tracking (Implied administrative function).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The duty of **loyalty** in the civil service requires officers to demonstrate dedication to:
options: The political party in power only.
The Government and its official policies.
Their ethnic group.
Their direct supervisor only.
questions.explanation: Loyalty ensures dedication to the government and its official policies.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: All official documents must be treated as the property of:
options: The Head of Department.
The Government.
The officer who generated them.
The requesting public entity.
questions.explanation: All official documents are government property, not the personal property of officers.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body ensures that civil service operations adhere to the rules outlined in the Handbook
options: The National Assembly.
The Ministry of Finance.
The Civil Service Commission.
The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC).
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission ensures that civil service operations adhere to the rules (Implied institutional role) Reference: rules.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Efficiency** in service delivery is stressed to ensure that government services:
options: Are kept highly confidential.
Meet public needs effectively and deliver value for money.
Favor specific regional interests.
Are delayed pending political approval.
questions.explanation: Efficiency ensures civil servants meet public needs effectively and deliver value for money.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The function of ensuring staff development and training is aligned with **MDA** goals is primarily the responsibility of the:
options: Permanent Secretary (in consultation with HR/Training units).
Chief Internal Auditor.
Union Executives.
Vote Controller.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary/Head of MDA is responsible for aligning staff development and training with organizational goals.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that official communication must pass through:
options: Social media platforms.
Personal contacts.
Established hierarchical channels.
Directly to the highest political office.
questions.explanation: All official communications must follow established hierarchical channels for proper documentation and accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The role of the Permanent Secretary includes ensuring adherence to financial regulations because they serve as the:
options: Chief Internal Auditor.
Statutory Head of the FCSC.
Accounting Officer.
Chairman of the National Assembly.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary is the Accounting Officer responsible for all financial transactions and compliance.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary role of the **Scheme of Service** (or 'Cadre')
options: To define leave entitlements.
To outline job roles, qualifications, and career progression pathways.
To track daily financial expenditure.
To record disciplinary proceedings.
questions.explanation: The Scheme of Service defines job roles, qualifications, and promotion pathways for staff.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Anonymity** guiding civil servants ensures that blame or praise for government policies is directed at:
options: The civil service bureaucracy.
The political leadership (Minister/President).
The Head of Civil Service.
The Budget Office.
questions.explanation: Anonymity ensures civil servants serve without seeking personal recognition; praise or blame is directed toward the political leadership.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Permanence** in the civil service serves to ensure:
options: Continuous loyalty to the ruling political party.
Continuity of policies and stability despite changes in political administration.
Fixed employment contracts for all staff.
Strict regional balancing in all appointments.
questions.explanation: Permanence ensures that the civil service continues its functions despite political changes.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The responsibility of implementing official directives and policy across **MDAs** through Circulars belongs primarily to the:
options: Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
Ministry of Justice.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC).
questions.explanation: The OHCSF issues Circulars that clarify policies and implement reforms across MDAs (Implied institutional role).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the **PSR** requirement regarding an officer's dedication during official working hours
options: They may run a small private business.
They must dedicate full time to official duties.
They should spend half the time on community service.
They are free to take on a second full-time paid job.
questions.explanation: Officers are expected to devote full time during official hours to official duties. Holding more than one full-time paid job is Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(r)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Delegation of Authority** in the Civil Service Handbook aims to improve efficiency while ensuring:
options: Junior officers take over all decision-making.
Ultimate responsibility remains with the senior delegating officer.
The process is kept highly secret.
All decisions are reversed upon political change.
questions.explanation: Delegation improves efficiency by allowing junior officers to act, but ultimate responsibility remains with the senior officer.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The statutory role of all employees of the Federal Government, including **Labour Union Executives** (**PSR** 020609), is to:
options: To discharge assigned duties efficiently and in line with service rules.
To coordinate annual budget preparation and warrant releases for the MDA.
To approve procurement awards on behalf of the Ministerial Tenders Board.
To conduct promotion interviews and confirmation examinations for all cadres.
questions.explanation: The primary responsibility of all employees (including Labour Union Executives) is to discharge the duties assigned to their employer in a timely and efficient manner (PSR 020609).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The process of **proper handover** during staff transfers is emphasized to maintain:
options: Secrecy and personal discretion.
Continuity of duties and accountability.
Regional dominance in the Ministry.
Quick processing of terminal benefits.
questions.explanation: Proper handover ensures continuity of duties and accountability in civil service operations.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The use of **Performance Appraisal Form (PAF)**, also known as APER, is the main tool used to assess an officer's competence and guide:
options: Recruitment of junior staff.
Sabbatical applications.
Promotion decisions.
Contract termination.
questions.explanation: The APER/PAF is the main assessment tool used to assess an officer's competence and guide promotion decisions.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The responsibility for identifying, initiating, and disseminating information about volunteer programmes for interns falls to the:
options: Federal Civil Service Commission.
Focal Office established in the OHCSF.
Ministry of Labour.
National Assembly.
questions.explanation: Focal Offices established in the OHCSF are responsible for identifying and disseminating information for volunteer and internship programmes (PSR 020417).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Transparency** in the civil service primarily supports:
options: Withholding of official records.
Open access to records and accountable decision-making.
Exclusion of the public from all meetings.
Favoritism in promotions.
questions.explanation: Transparency requires that decisions and processes are open, with records available for accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires the **Performance Management Cycle** (**PSR** 050301) to run for what duration
options: Six months.
January to December of every year.
Four years.
The entire duration of the officer's service.
questions.explanation: The Performance Management Cycle shall run from January to December of every year.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Minister** controls Parastatals at what level, according to the **PSR**'
options: Day-to-day management level.
Policy level only.
Staff promotion level.
Direct procurement level.
questions.explanation: A Minister exercises control of Parastatals at the policy level only through the Board of the Parastatal (PSR 170201).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Departmental Promotion Committee (DPC)** is primarily responsible for:
options: Issuing final dismissal letters.
Conducting promotion interviews and recommending eligible staff.
Managing the MDA's bank accounts.
Handling staff transfers across Ministries.
questions.explanation: The DPC screens, conducts interviews, and recommends eligible staff for promotion (Implied institutional role).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: The **Junior Staff Committee (JSC)** handles all appointments, promotions, and discipline matters for officers on which grade levels?
options: GL 15 and above.
All officers on contract appointment.
GL 07 and above.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (GL 01–06.). The Junior Staff Committee handles appointments, promotions, and discipline for officers on GL01–06 (PSR 170301).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** encourages continuous professional development to ensure civil servants:
options: Remain static in their knowledge base.
Adapt to modern practices and improve efficiency.
Engage in political campaigns.
Reduce their accountability.
questions.explanation: Professional development aims to enhance knowledge, skills, attitude, effectiveness, and efficiency of staff (PSR 070101).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which institution provides training and capacity development for civil servants in Nigeria
options: Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
Administrative Staff College of Nigeria (ASCON).
National Universities Commission (NUC).
Debt Management Office (DMO).
questions.explanation: ASCON (Administrative Staff College of Nigeria) provides training and capacity development for civil servants.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Senior Staff Committee (SSC)** is the oversight body for HR decisions concerning officers on which grade levels
options: GL 07 and above.
Only GL 17.
Officers on contract terms only.
questions.explanation: The Senior Staff Committee handles decisions for officers on GL07 and above (PSR 170301).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which administrative practice ensures staff receive their full entitlements promptly upon transfer
options: Ignoring the transfer letter.
Failure to document the movement.
Proper processing of posting entitlements (PSR 020607).
Transferring without OHCSF approval.
questions.explanation: The cost of entitlements due to the employee whose posting requires a change of duty station shall be borne by the posting MDA (PSR 020607).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Why is **Teamwork** emphasized in the Civil Service Handbook
options: To create unnecessary competition among officers.
To foster collaboration and enhance collective efficiency and service delivery.
To encourage high staff turnover.
To reduce ministerial accountability.
questions.explanation: Teamwork fosters collaboration and enhances service delivery within the civil service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the officer’s responsibility regarding changes in their marital status (**PSR** practice)
options: They must notify only the Federal Civil Service Commission directly.
They must report changes in marital status through appropriate channel within the required period.
They may wait until the next promotion exercise to declare the change.
They should update the record only during retirement processing.
questions.explanation: An officer must report changes in marital status within the required period to update official records (Implied administrative practice).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires the **Promotion Committees** (DPC/SSC) to base their decisions on:
options: Political patronage and regional balance.
Merit, performance, and absence of disciplinary issues.
The officer's age and length of stay in the previous station.
Only the number of training courses attended.
questions.explanation: Promotion is determined by merit, performance, vacancy, and eligibility, and absence of disciplinary issues (PSR 020802(d)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The Head of Department's duty in the payment process is to certify what before payment is made to a contractor
options: The budget Virement Warrant.
That services have been duly performed and accepted.
The officer’s annual leave roster.
The contractor’s political affiliation.
questions.explanation: The Head of Department certifies that services have been duly performed before payments are made,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The Permanent Secretary/CEO concerning adverse comments on an officer's conduct (**PSR** 050205) must convey the report in sympathetic terms, with the objective of:
options: Dismissing the officer immediately.
Enabling and encouraging the officer to overcome shortcomings.
Ignoring the misconduct entirely.
Making the report public knowledge.
questions.explanation: Adverse comments must be conveyed in sympathetic terms to enable and encourage the officer to overcome shortcomings (PSR 050205).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** Chapter provides guidelines for training and staff development within and outside Nigeria
options: Chapter 4.
Chapter 7.
Chapter 10.
Chapter 12.
questions.explanation: Chapter 7 of the PSR covers Training and Staff Development within and outside Nigeria.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the significance of the **Annual Financial Statement**'
options: It defines the annual budget allocation.
It reports on government’s financial performance within the fiscal year.
It certifies contract completion.
It dictates the officer's promotion timeline.
questions.explanation: The Annual Financial Statement reports on government’s financial performance within the fiscal year.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The civil service values of Integrity, Neutrality, Service, Discipline, and Accountability are emphasized in which Handbook section
options: The Preamble.
Ethics and Values (CSH).
The Appointment Circular.
The Discipline Chapter only.
questions.explanation: Core values including integrity, neutrality, service, discipline, and accountability are found in the Ethics and Values section of the Civil Service Handbook.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The rule for retiring officers being re-engaged on contract (**PSR** 020412) states that their remuneration shall be based on:
options: Their old salary and pension only.
Negotiable terms by the parties concerned.
The highest possible salary grade level.
A fixed rate set by the FCSC for all retirees.
questions.explanation: Remuneration for non-Public/Civil Servants re-engaged after retirement shall be based on negotiable terms by the parties concerned (PSR 020412).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The overall responsibility for coordinating inter-ministerial postings and service-wide reforms rests with the:
options: Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
Secretary to the Government of the Federation (SGF).
Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
Ministry of Budget and National Planning.
questions.explanation: The OHCSF handles deployments, coordination of postings, and overall service reforms (Implied institutional role).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the **Reward and Recognition System (R&RS)**'
options: To replace the promotion process entirely.
To institutionalize a culture of excellence and motivate staff.
To enforce disciplinary measures.
To calculate retirement benefits.
questions.explanation: The R&RS aims to institutionalize a culture of excellence and motivate staff by recognizing meritorious service (Implied component of performance management).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 070103** mandates strategies to ensure continuous capacity development shall be encapsulated in the:
options: Annual Leave Roster.
Public Service Training and Capacity Development Policy.
Official Secrets Act.
Financial Regulations.
questions.explanation: Continuous capacity development strategies must be in the Public Service Training and Capacity Development Policy (PSR 070103),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule requires all newly employed staff to sign an Oath of Secrecy (or Security)
questions.explanation: PSR 020210 makes it the duty of the Permanent Secretary to ensure newly employed staff sign an Oath of Secrecy.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The concept of **Meritocracy** in the civil service requires that appointments and promotions must be based on:
options: Seniority and political connections.
Competence, credibility, and equal employment opportunity.
Regional background only.
Personal relationships with the Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Appointments and promotions should ensure merit, credibility, and equal employment opportunity (PSR 170302).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Duties & Responsibilities, which action best demonstrates duties & responsibilities governance
options: Apply approved duties & responsibilities procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved duties & responsibilities procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in duties & responsibilities Reference: Duties & Responsibilities - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in duties & responsibilities Reference: Duties & Responsibilities - Expansion Set.

Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
