questions.question: Within government administration, which office retains the ultimate duty for the control of votes to the Federal Executive Council?
options: The Accountant-General.
Officers controlling votes. □
The Minister of Finance.
Accounting Officers.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Accounting Officers.). Financial Regulation 401 (ii) states: "the ultimate accountability to the Federal Executive Council for the control of votes rests with the Account log Officers at all times."
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, choose the best answer for: Can an officer to whom expenditure control has been delegated sub-delegate such accountability?
options: No, never.
Yes, freely.
Yes, with the approval of the Accounting Officer.
Only for minor expenses. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (No, never.). Financial Regulation 404 states: "He must not sub-delegate such accountability wholly or partly, without the knowledge and approval of the officer controlling the vote."
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: What type of control is emphasized for the collection of all revenues under the accountability of the Accounting Officer?
options: External control.
Informal control. Q
Centralized control.
Internal control.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Internal control.). Financial Regulation 1603(g) specifically highlights an "adequate system of Internal control".
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, which option aligns with the meaning of specifically forbidden in handling money received in the course of official duties by an officer who is not a Revenue Collector or Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: Supporting lodgement with a paying-in form. □
Delaying their lodgement.
Issuing an official receipt.
Lodging them with a Sub-Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Delaying their lodgement.). Financial Regulation 218 states that such monies must be lodged "without delay".
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: The Administrative and Professional Officers' duties are derived from what?
options: The needs of the public.Q.
The system of Government.
The directives of the Permanent Secretary.
The financial regulations.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The system of Government.). The raison d'etre and duties of the Administrative and the Professional Officers derive from our system of Government.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: How should the deadlines for service delivery be determined?
options: In an arbitrary manner.
By the political party in power.a.
By the whims of the head of department.
In a realistic manner and must be communicated to users27.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (In a realistic manner and must be communicated to users27.). Such deadlines must be determined in a realistic manner, and must be communicated to users of public services.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, select the statement that correctly defines the accountability of officers controlling votes regarding payment for services rendered?
options: To defer payments to the next financial year for budgetary reasons.
To only payment for services after all claims have been perfectly validated.
To secure payments are settled within the financial year in which they were rendered.
To transfer all outstanding claims to a suspense account
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To secure payments are settled within the financial year in which they were rendered.). Financial Regulation 412(i) states: "Officers controlling votes are accountable for' ensuring that, as far as possible, payment for services rendered are settled within the financial year in which they were rendered."
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes a key duty of the Chief Executive of a Parastatal to the Board?
options: To only report to the Minister.
To refuse to implement decisions that are not to his liking.
To manage the Board's personal affairs. □..
To implement the decisions and policies of the Board.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To implement the decisions and policies of the Board.). The Chief Executive is expected to implement the decisions and policies of the Board.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: The Principle of Accountability states that public officers must be personally accountable for what?
options: Their individual actions or omissions in the performance of their duties.
The actions of the government as a whole. □.
The actions of their colleagues.
The decisions of the Minister.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Their individual actions or omissions in the performance of their duties.). The Principle of Accountability states that public officers must be personally accountable for their individual actions or omissions in the performance of their duties, and must respond, where necessary, to their superiors.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option best describes the quota reserved for recruitment of persons with disabilities?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (5%). Rule 020202(b) reserves five percent (5%) of all recruitments for persons with disabilities.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, which option best describes one of the Accountant-General's duties regarding compliance with rules and regulations?
options: To manage all federal government investments.3
To conduct routine and in-depth inspection of books of accounts to guarantee compliance.
To approve all government contracts.
To issue warnings to defaulting officers.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To conduct routine and in-depth inspection of books of accounts to guarantee compliance.). Financial Regulation 107(h) states the Accountant-General must "conduct routine and in-depth inspection of the books of accounts of federal ministries... to guarantee compliance with rules, regulations, and policy decisions."
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option best describes the procedure for deposits held in foreign currency?
options: They are the duty of the Central Bank of Nigeria. □
They are not subject to these regulations.
They must be converted to local currency immediately.
They should be treated in the same manner as deposits in local currency.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They should be treated in the same manner as deposits in local currency.). While not explicitly stated, the general principle of the financial regulations is that all government funds are subject to proper accounting procedures, regardless of the currency. The regulations for deposits apply universally.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes the duty of the members of the Board of Survey regarding the report?
options: Only the Chairman is in charge for the report's accuracy.
They are not in charge for the contents of the report.
They are jointly accountable for the accuracy of the report.
They are only in charge for the physical counting
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They are jointly accountable for the accuracy of the report.). The fact that the report must be signed by all members implies that they are jointly accountable for its accuracy.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: which option aligns with the meaning of the implication of the Board of Survey's findings for an officer-in-charge?
options: The officer is solely accountable for any discrepancies.
The officer is immediately dismissed if a shortage is found.
The officer's accountability is discharged only after a satisfactory report is submitted.3
The findings have no consequences for the officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The officer is solely accountable for any discrepancies.). The officer-in-charge must be present during the count and the Board's report is submitted for investigation of discrepancies, which implies the officer is in charge for the items under their charge. Part 3: Financial Regulations book: Pension Scheme in the Federal Public Service
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: Which of these is an exclusive accountability of the Federal Government, according to the handbook?
options: Roads □
Education
Health
Foreign Affairs
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Foreign Affairs). The Federal Government has exclusive accountability for specified matters such as Foreign Affairs.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: Non-tax revenue refers to income accruable to the government outside of its taxes, such as:?
options: Import duties
Operating surplus from its parastatals
Purchase tax Q
Excise duties
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Operating surplus from its parastatals). The non-tax revenue refers to income accruable to the government outside its taxes such as operating surplus from its parastatals.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: choose the best answer for: The accountability for the Federal Budget is shared between which two arms of the Federal Government?
options: The Executive and the Legislature
The Judiciary and the Legislature 379
The Executive and the Judiciary
The President and the Chief Justice Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Executive and the Legislature). The accountability for the Federal Budget is shared between the Executive and the Legislature, which are two of the three arms of the Federal Government
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: What fiscal rule is observed under the Fiscal duty Act 2007?
options: Total revenue must always exceed total expenditure.
Total revenue is not a factor in determining total expenditure
Total expenditure must not exceed total revenue.
Total expenditure can exceed total revenue if there are external grants.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Total expenditure must not exceed total revenue.). The fiscal rule being observed under the Fiscal duty Act 2007 is that total expenditure must not exceed total revenue
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: choose the best answer for: The Manpower Development Office (MDO) has the duty for coordinating what?
options: The pool system for senior officers.
The deployment of staff.a.
Service-wide administration of training and development.
Preparation of draft Council memoranda on HRM matters.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Service-wide administration of training and development.). The MDO has the duty for ensuring the Service has its fair share of quality manpower and in this context, the Office coordinates Service-wide administration of training and development. 420 Section C Pat 1: Management of Human Resources in the Civil Service
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: choose the best answer for: What should a Civil Servant who has served in a particular Ministry for a very long time avoid doing?
options: Assuming that their experience is the only thing that matters.
Sharing institutional knowledge. Q.
Giving advice to the Minister.
Offering useful suggestions for improvements.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Assuming that their experience is the only thing that matters.). Long-serving Officers in a particular Ministry should not become so set in their own ways as to assume that their experience is the only thing that matters.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: What should adjustments to the way services are organized and operated lead to?
options: Increased costs.a.
Better service delivery.
Fewer services for the public.
More bureaucracy.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Better service delivery.). Adjustments to the way in which services are organized and operated must lead to better service delivery and must be informed by best practices in the application of the information and communication technologies.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: A key principle of service delivery is to be responsive. What does this mean?
options: Only serving those who are powerful.
Only serving those who ask for help.
Delaying services as much as possible. □.
Serving all stakeholders within a reasonable timeframe.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Serving all stakeholders within a reasonable timeframe.). The principle of Responsiveness states that institutions and processes try to serve all stakeholders within a reasonable timeframe.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which role should receive a copy of a handing-over note?
options: The public.Q.
The Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.
The officer's predecessor.
The officer's friends and family. 450
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.). A copy of the handing-over note is to be sent to the Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: select the option that best answers: The workflow of creating a new file should be guided by what?
options: The needs of the officer.
The opinion of the Head of Department. Q.
The existing File Index.
The number of documents.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The existing File Index.). New files should be created based on the existing File Index to secure the consistency and logic of the file numbering system.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the aim of a "Memorandum" in the Civil Service?
options: To provide a confidential report.
To document a personal conversation.
To communicate with the public.3.
To communicate internally within the same department or Ministry.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To communicate internally within the same department or Ministry.). A memorandum is a type of correspondence used to communicate internally between officers within the same department or Ministry.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The disciplinary process for civil servants in the Nigerian Public Service is outlined in which chapter of the **PSR**'
options: Chapter 5 (Performance).
Chapter 10 (Discipline).
Chapter 12 (Leave).
Chapter 14 (Allowances).
questions.explanation: The disciplinary process for civil servants is outlined in Chapter 10 of the PSR.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The first step in the disciplinary procedure for misconduct is usually:
options: Interdiction (half pay).
Suspension (no pay).
Issuance of a written query.
Dismissal.
questions.explanation: The disciplinary process typically begins with the issuance of a written query (PSR 100307),,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Misconduct involving lateness, insubordination, negligence, or inappropriate dressing is classified as:
options: General Inefficiency (Rule 100201).
Misconduct (Rule 100301).
Serious Misconduct (Rule 100401).
Criminal Offence.
questions.explanation: Improper behavior like insubordination or habitual lateness is defined as Misconduct (PSR 100301).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which offense is classified as **Serious Misconduct** that may lead to dismissal
options: Minor lateness to duty.
Absence without leave for 3 days.
Fraud, corruption, or unauthorized disclosure of official information.
Failure to attend an optional training course.
questions.explanation: Serious wrongdoing, including corruption, and unauthorized disclosure of official information,,, constitutes Serious Misconduct (PSR 100401).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Falsification of official records constitutes which level of misconduct under the Civil Service Handbook
options: Minor misconduct.
Gross misconduct.
Performance lapse.
Training gap.
questions.explanation: Falsification of official records is considered gross misconduct.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer who is absent from duty without permission for 14 consecutive days may be deemed by the Service to have:
options: Resigned from the service (PSR 030413).
Been suspended on full pay.
Been promoted.
Been granted special leave.
questions.explanation: Unauthorized absence for 14 consecutive days is construed as resignation from the service (PSR 030413, implied rule reference).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer under **Suspension** pending investigation into serious misconduct receives what salary status
options: Full pay.
Half pay (Interdiction).
No salary (Suspension without pay).
Quarter pay.
questions.explanation: Suspension (pending case conclusion for gross offenses) is usually without salary (PSR 100406, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which penalty for financial misconduct is intended to recover the exact amount of financial loss caused by the officer's negligence
options: Demotion.
Surcharge.
Interdiction.
Dismissal.
questions.explanation: Surcharge is the recovery of government losses from the officer's pay, applied when the loss is due to the officer’s fault.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Disciplinary action for misconduct may require an internal investigation by a **Board of Inquiry** consisting of at least:
options: One member.
Two members.
Three members.
Five members.
questions.explanation: Allegations of misconduct are typically investigated by a Board of Inquiry with at least three members to ensure impartiality and fairness (Implied due process requirement).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Insubordination is defined as:
options: Working overtime without approval.
Refusal to obey lawful instructions from superiors.
Lateness to duty.
Seeking promotion.
questions.explanation: Insubordination means refusing to obey lawful instructions from superiors.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The ultimate disciplinary penalty for Serious Misconduct resulting in permanent removal and forfeiture of benefits is:
options: Compulsory retirement.
Reduction in rank.
Interdiction.
Dismissal from service.
questions.explanation: Dismissal is the ultimate penalty for serious misconduct, resulting in permanent removal and forfeiture of most benefits.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 100409** mandates that an officer charged with a criminal offense must promptly report the charge to:
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts in the Ministry.
His Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Chair of the Ministerial Disciplinary Committee.
questions.explanation: An Officer must promptly report a criminal charge to his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office (PSR 100409, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is prohibited as a disciplinary punishment under the **PSR**'
options: Reduction in rank.
Surcharge.
Imposition of fines.
Withholding of increment.
questions.explanation: The PSR explicitly prohibits the imposition of fines as a disciplinary punishment (Implied rule reference).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer under **Interdiction** receives what salary status
options: Full salary.
Half salary.
No salary (Suspension).
Only basic allowances.
questions.explanation: During interdiction (temporary suspension pending investigation), an officer is placed on half pay (PSR 100404, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The intentional act of using government vehicles or property for private business is classified as:
options: Minor misconduct.
Gross misconduct (or Serious Misconduct).
Acceptable practice.
Administrative lapse.
questions.explanation: Using government property, such as vehicles, for private business is considered gross misconduct.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the penalty for an officer who fails the compulsory confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts (**PSR** 030104)
options: Required to resign or be withdrawn from the service.
Demotion in rank.
Suspension for six months.
Transfer to a remote station.
questions.explanation: An Officer who fails the confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts shall be required to resign or be withdrawn from the service (PSR 030104).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Contravention of the prohibition on membership of a Secret Society (**PSR** 020211) is regarded as:
options: Minor Misconduct.
Serious Misconduct.
General Inefficiency.
Grievance.
questions.explanation: Contravention of the prohibition against secret societies is regarded as an act of serious misconduct (PSR 020212, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body holds the final authority on matters of discipline and appeal for senior civil servants (**GL 07** and above)
options: The Minister.
The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The Office of the Head of Civil Service (OHCSF).
The Accountant-General.
questions.explanation: The FCSC has final authority on appointment, promotion, and discipline for senior officers.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An aggrieved officer appealing a disciplinary decision (Chapter 11) must generally have the appeal handled within a maximum period of:
options: 30 days.
Three months.
Six months.
One year.
questions.explanation: An appeal submitted more than six months after the decision will generally not be entertained unless the delay is explained (PSR 110209(b)), implying a six-month window.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ground for rejecting a petition or appeal (**PSR** 110208)
options: The petition is illegible.
The matter is pending before a court of law.
The petition contains the required signature and staff number.
The petition repeats previous claims without new facts.
questions.explanation: A petition must contain the required authentication details (signature, name, number) to be accepted. Grounds for rejection include illegibility, pending court case, repetition, or abusive language,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Under **PSR** **Rule 040202**, an officer's increment may be deferred or withheld on grounds of:
options: Unsatisfactory performance or being under interdiction/suspension.
Attending approved training courses.
Transfer to another Ministry.
Receiving a promotion.
questions.explanation: An increment shall normally be granted unless the officer is on interdiction/suspension or has a disciplinary action pending, or on account of poor performance (PSR 040202).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Engaging in **partisan political activities** is classified as:
options: Misconduct (Rule 100301).
Serious Misconduct (Rule 100402(f)).
General Inefficiency (Rule 100201).
Acceptable political expression.
questions.explanation: Engaging in partisan political activities is listed as Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(f), implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: If an **MDA** discovers new misconduct during an ongoing disciplinary inquiry, what must happen before punishment can be applied
options: The officer must be dismissed immediately.
The new offense must be ignored.
The officer must be informed and allowed to respond to the new allegation.
The Board of Inquiry must be disbanded.
questions.explanation: The officer must be informed and given the opportunity to defend themselves (implied by due process and natural justice, PSR 100307).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes **General Inefficiency** (**PSR** **Rule 100201**)
options: A single instance of fraud.
A pattern of omissions or incompetence showing inability to perform duties.
Refusal to obey a lawful order.
Unauthorized disclosure of confidential information.
questions.explanation: General Inefficiency consists of a series of omissions or incompetence, the cumulative effect of which shows that the Officer is not capable of discharging efficiently the duties of the office he holds (PSR 100201).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In deciding whether to withhold or defer an increment (**PSR** 040204), the Permanent Secretary must take into account:
options: The officer’s social media usage.
The gravity of the shortcoming and the quality of the officer's previous service.
The officer's tribal affiliation.
The size of the MDA's annual budget.
questions.explanation: The decision must weigh the gravity of the shortcoming against the quality of the officer's previous service, as withholding is a serious penalty.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** forbids officers from seeking to influence prominent persons to gain promotion or transfer (**PSR** 100427), aiming to enforce the principle of:
options: Anonymity.
Meritocracy.
Nepotism.
Neutrality.
questions.explanation: PSR 100427 prohibits seeking influence to ensure appointments and promotions are based on merit.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Misconduct involving the use of official information for personal financial advantage is prohibited under the ethical rule of:
options: Political neutrality.
Conflict of Interest.
Domestic Preference.
Official Secrets Act exemption.
questions.explanation: Officers must avoid conflicts of interest, such as using official information for personal gain Reference: rule of.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who has the authority to issue a written query to a Director (**GL 17**) in a Ministry
options: The Head of the Procurement Unit.
The Permanent Secretary.
The Junior Staff Committee.
The Accounts Clerk.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary/CEO, as the head of the MDA and disciplinary authority up to delegated levels, issues queries to senior officers within the Ministry (PSR 100307),,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body is responsible for enforcing ethical standards in public office by receiving and verifying public officers’ **asset declarations**'
options: The EFCC.
The FCSC.
The Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB).
The Auditor-General.
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB) receives and verifies public officers’ asset declarations (Constitutional mandate).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The maximum period for the deferment of an officer's increment must not exceed:
options: Four months.
Six months.
Nine months.
One year.
questions.explanation: The period for deferred increment must not be less than three months nor more than six months (PSR 040205),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: If an officer is required to sign a **bond** for specialized training, the purpose is to ensure:
options: The officer receives automatic promotion after training.
They do not retire before completing the bond service commitment.
The officer can accept external employment during the bond period.
The training institution assumes all post-training deployment decisions.
questions.explanation: Bonds (PSR 070205) are required for specialized training to ensure service commitment upon completion.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What happens if a female officer interrupts a course of training of **not more than six months duration** on grounds of pregnancy (**PSR** 070210)
options: The officer receives immediate dismissal.
The officer must refund the whole or part of the cost of the course.
The officer is granted permanent study leave.
The officer must be promoted immediately.
questions.explanation: A female officer who interrupts a course of training of not more than six months duration shall be called upon to refund the whole or part of the cost of the course (PSR 070210).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The prohibition on receiving or granting **preferential treatment** (Nepotism) in appointments is classified as:
options: Misconduct (Rule 100301).
Serious Misconduct (Rule 100402(t)).
General Inefficiency.
Acceptable practice for senior staff.
questions.explanation: Nepotism is listed as an act of Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(t), implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Under the **PSR**, an officer's discipline can be applied to:
options: Only work-related conduct during official hours.
Only off-duty conduct.
Both work-related and off-duty conduct that affects the service.
The officer's family members only.
questions.explanation: Discipline can apply to both work-related and off-duty conduct that affects the service (Implied scope of PSR Chapter 10).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which officer is empowered to initiate disciplinary action by issuing a query for misconduct
options: Any colleague.
The Messenger.
The officer's supervisor or Head of Department.
An External Consultant.
questions.explanation: Only officers in supervisory roles (or the Permanent Secretary/CEO) can initiate disciplinary action by issuing a query (PSR 100307),,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: If an officer’s salary is withheld due to unauthorized absence, the sanction is specifically governed by which **PSR** Rule
options: Rule 040101 (FR Authority).
Rule 040206 (Salary Withholding).
Rule 100401 (Serious Misconduct).
Rule 120203 (Annual Leave).
questions.explanation: Rule 040206 allows salary to be withheld in cases of unauthorized absence (Rule 040206).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The imposition of progressive discipline for repeated minor offenses typically starts with:
options: Immediate dismissal.
A formal written warning.
Suspension.
Reduction in rank.
questions.explanation: Repeated minor offenses attract progressive discipline, starting with warnings (Implied procedural requirement).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that officers avoid all forms of **Sexual Harassment**, classified under:
options: PSR Chapter 5 (Performance).
PSR Chapter 10 (Discipline).
PSR Chapter 12 (Leave).
PSR Chapter 15 (Ethics).
questions.explanation: Sexual harassment is strictly prohibited under the Code of Conduct and is a serious disciplinary matter (PSR Chapter 10 context).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who must approve a petition before it is considered by the appropriate authority (**PSR** practice)
options: The officer’s union leader.
The officer's immediate supervisor (through proper channels).
The Minister of Justice.
The External Auditor.
questions.explanation: Complaints/grievances must be submitted through the appropriate channel, usually starting with the immediate superior (PSR 020606),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: The **PSR** permits an officer to appeal a disciplinary decision through the proper channels as described in:?
options: Chapter 15 (Inventions).
Chapter 13 (Housing).
Chapter 5 (Performance).
Chapter 11 (Petitions and Appeals).
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Chapter 11 (Petitions and Appeals).). Aggrieved officers can petition decisions through the proper channels as described in Chapter 11 (Petitions and Appeals),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer under investigation may be transferred to another **MDA**. This action is taken under the authority of:
options: The FCSC only.
The disciplinary authority (PSR 110205, implied).
The Minister of Works.
The National Assembly.
questions.explanation: Officers under investigation may be transferred, subject to FCSC consultation (PSR 110205 context).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The period for which a deferred increment must be fixed is not less than three months nor more than:
options: Four months.
Six months.
Nine months.
One year.
questions.explanation: The deferment period must not be less than three months nor more than six months (PSR 040205),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** prohibits the use of which platform for official communication
options: Memo format.
Official Circulars.
Established hierarchical channels.
Social media platforms.
questions.explanation: Official communication must follow hierarchical channels; social media is generally prohibited for official correspondence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that all financial misconduct leading to dismissal must be supported by:
options: Public opinion polls.
A Board of Inquiry report and internal investigation findings.
The officer’s personal assets.
A Ministerial decree only.
questions.explanation: Dismissal for misconduct must be supported by investigation findings following due process (PSR 100305, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When an officer is charged with Misconduct, the disciplinary query must contain
options: The officer's medical history.
Details of the offense, the broken rule, and the likely penalty.
A list of all previous employers.
The Auditor-General's report.
questions.explanation: The query must be precise, relating to the circumstances of the offense, the rule broken, and the likely penalty (PSR 100307).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** rule that mandates officers cannot be punished without being heard ensures adherence to the principle of:
options: Anonymity.
Natural Justice.
Permanence.
Partisanship.
questions.explanation: The right to defend oneself (PSR 100307) ensures natural justice is upheld.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which agency is primarily responsible for investigating and prosecuting serious corruption cases involving public officers
options: The FCSC.
The EFCC.
The Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB).
The National Assembly.
questions.explanation: The EFCC (Economic and Financial Crimes Commission) investigates and prosecutes financial crimes and corruption cases.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The prohibition on **holding more than one full-time paid job** (**PSR** 100402(r)) is classified as:
options: General Inefficiency.
Misconduct.
Serious Misconduct.
Minor Misconduct.
questions.explanation: Holding more than one full-time paid job is listed as an act of Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(r), implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Disciplinary action resulting in **Demotion** (Reduction in Rank) is listed under which category of sanctions
options: General Inefficiency measures.
Misconduct measures (PSR 100801, implied).
Only for officers on probation.
Criminal prosecution measures.
questions.explanation: Demotion (Reduction in Rank) is one of the penalties prescribed under the disciplinary measures outlined in PSR Chapter 10 (Implied sanction).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The final authority to discipline Directors (**GL 17**) in the Federal Civil Service rests with the:
options: Permanent Secretary.
Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
Minister.
Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The FCSC is the disciplinary authority for senior officers, including the Directorate Cadre.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Discipline & Conduct, which action best demonstrates discipline & conduct governance
options: Apply approved discipline & conduct procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved discipline & conduct procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective discipline & conduct, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure discipline & conduct compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on discipline & conduct risk management within Discipline & Conduct
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling discipline & conduct, which choice reflects proper administrative ethics standards
options: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain grievance handling in Discipline & Conduct
options: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Discipline & Conduct, which action best demonstrates performance management
options: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.

options: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on anti-corruption safeguards within Discipline & Conduct
options: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.

options: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.

options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.

options: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.

options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.

options: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.

Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.

options: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.

options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
