Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Maintain accurate files and update status at each control point. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on performance standards within Discipline & Conduct
options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling discipline & conduct, which choice reflects proper discipline & conduct governance standards
options: Apply approved discipline & conduct procedures and maintain complete records.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Apply approved discipline & conduct procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain discipline & conduct compliance in Discipline & Conduct
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Discipline & Conduct, which action best demonstrates discipline & conduct risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective discipline & conduct, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure administrative ethics
options: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on grievance handling within Discipline & Conduct
options: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling discipline & conduct, which choice reflects proper performance management standards
options: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain discipline and conduct in Discipline & Conduct
options: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: select the option that best answers: How does the Attendance Register distinguish between staff who arrive on time and those who are late?
options: A red line is drawn across the register, and latecomers sign below it.
A supervisor makes a note next to the latecomer's name. Q.
There is no distinction; everyone signs in the same place.
Latecomers have to sign on a different sheet. 434
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (A red line is drawn across the register, and latecomers sign below it.). Not later than 8.00 a.m., a red line should be drawn across the register each day so that staff who come late have to sign below the red line
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, which option aligns with the meaning of the aim of passing the filed original of the Handing Over Notes to the Supervisor and/or a superior officer?
options: To keep it confidential.
To discard it. □.
To guarantee that the duties of that post have been properly taken over.
To allow them to rewrite it.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To guarantee that the duties of that post have been properly taken over.). The filed original should then be passed to the Supervisor and/or a superior officer who should know that the duties of that post have been properly taken over. 491
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which office should an officer not communicate with on official business, outside of the proper channel?
options: The Head of Department.
A junior officer.
A more senior officer.
A non-official or non-Government person.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (A non-official or non-Government person.a.). The golden rule is that an officer must not communicate on official business with a non-official or non-Government person unless the officer is specifically directed to do so by his superior officer.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: What does the term "Minute/Memo" mean?
options: A personal note.a.
A short report for a supervisor.
A written submission to present a view or position on an issue.
A summary of a meeting.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (A written submission to present a view or position on an issue.). Minute/Memo:- Written submission to present a view or position on an issue.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: Is an officer who is a member of the Examination Board automatically eligible to receive invigilator or examiner fees?
options: Yes, as long as they are a director.
Yes, all members are eligible.
No, only those who perform the duties of invigilation or examination are eligible.
The rules are silent on this
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (No, only those who perform the duties of invigilation or examination are eligible.). The fees are for the performance of the duties of invigilation and examining, not merely for being on the board.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: which role is the topmost career Civil Servant in a Local Government set-up?
options: Councilor
Secretary
Mayora
Chairman
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Secretary). The topmost career Civil Servant in the set-up is designated as Secretary
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: According to the handbook, what is the best way to treat a new Civil Servant joining a Ministry?
options: Subject them to a probationary period without any support.
Give them the most difficult tasks to test their resolve. □.
Ignore them and let them learn on their own.
Receive them with kindness and courtesy, and assist them to settle down.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Receive them with kindness and courtesy, and assist them to settle down.). New Officers joining a Ministry should be received with kindness and courtesy and should be assisted in every way to settle down.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the danger of a Civil Servant accepting gifts from contractors and business people?
options: It is a normal part of business. □.
It could compromise the integrity of the Civil Servant.
It will create a good relationship.
It is an act of kindness.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (It could compromise the integrity of the Civil Servant.). The Civil Servant should remember that the acceptance of gifts from contractors and business people could compromise his position and his integrity
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: What does the principle of "diligence" demand from a Civil Servant?
options: To be a hardworking and conscientious officer who gives his best to the job. 427
To be lazy and apathetic.
To delegate all tasks to subordinates.Q.
To only work during official hours.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To be a hardworking and conscientious officer who gives his best to the job. 427). Every Civil Servant is expected to be a hardworking and conscientious officer who gives his best to the job
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, select the statement that correctly defines the implication of failing to take reasonable care of documents entrusted to a Civil Servant?
options: It is a normal part of the job.Q.
It is an offence.
It is a minor infraction.
It is an excusable mistake.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (It is an offence.). It is an offence for a Civil Servant to fail to take reasonable care of any documents entrusted to his care.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: The handbook advises Civil Servants to avoid what when dealing with the public?
options: Asking for gifts or favors. Q. 432
Helping them when possible.
Being helpful, but still being impartial.
Attending to their requests promptly.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Asking for gifts or favors. Q. 432). It is a disgrace for a Civil Servant to tout for official matters and demand money or gifts from members of the public.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: What should a Civil Servant do with a file when they are finished with it?
options: Throw it away if it is not needed anymore.
Keep it in their desk for future reference.
Put it on a "bring-up" pad if action is necessitate at a later date.
Pass it to a colleague without informing them.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Put it on a "bring-up" pad if action is necessitate at a later date.). When an officer has finished with a file, he should secure that it is returned to the registry. If a file is necessitate at a later date, it should be put on a "bring-up" pad.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the role of an "official letter" in the Civil Service?
options: To provide a confidential report. □.
To communicate internally.
To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
To document a personal conversation.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To communicate with other Ministries and the public.). An official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the general public.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the statement that correctly defines the consequence of failing to take reasonable care of documents entrusted to a Civil Servant?
options: It is a normal part of the job. 457 Q.
It is an offense.
It is a minor infraction.
It is an excusable mistake.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (It is an offense.). It is an offense for a Civil Servant to fail to take reasonable care of any documents entrusted to his care.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: How should a Civil Servant handle a file that has been left on their desk and has been forgotten?
options: Throw it away.
Read the contents to see if it is important.a.
Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.
Keep it in their desk.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.). Should a file be left on a desk and has been forgotten, it should be reported to the superior officer and returned to the registry.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: The handbook advises Civil Servants to avoid leaving what on their desks at the end of the day?
options: Their lunch box.
Their personal belongings.
Official files and documents.
Their phone. □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Official files and documents.). Documents and files must not be left lying on desks where they could get lost or misplaced, or read by unauthorized persons.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: What should a Civil Servant do with a file that has been on their desk for a long time without any action?
options: Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.
Keep it in their desk.
Throw it away.
Pass it to a colleague. □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.). Should a file be left on a desk for a long time without any action, it should be reported to the superior officer and returned to the registry.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: How should a Civil Servant handle a file that has been on their desk for a long time without any action?
options: Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.
Throw it away.
Pass it to a colleague.a.
Keep it in their desk.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.). Should a file be left on a desk for a long time without any action, it should be reported to the superior officer and returned to the registry.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, which option best describes the aim of an "official letter" in the Civil Service?
options: To communicate with other Ministries and the public97.
To provide a confidential report. Q.
To document a personal conversation.
To communicate internally.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To communicate with other Ministries and the public97.). Letters to other Ministries are always addressed to the Honourable Minister. An official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the general public.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option best describes considered a very serious offense for a Civil Servant?
options: Taking a day off.
Arriving late to a meeting.
Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties121.
Talking to a colleague.3.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties121.). Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties is a very serious offence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the statement that correctly defines the aim of an "official letter" in the Civil Service?
options: To document a personal conversation.
To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
To provide a confidential report.a.
To communicate internally.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To communicate with other Ministries and the public.). Letters to other Ministries are always addressed to the Honourable Minister. An official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the general public
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of considered a very serious offense for a Civil Servant?
options: Talking to a colleague. □.
Taking a day off.
Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties162.
Arriving late to a meeting.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties162.). Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties is a very serious offence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the objective of an "official letter" in the Civil Service?
options: To document a personal conversation.
To communicate with other Ministries and the public.
To provide a confidential report. □.
To communicate internally.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To communicate with other Ministries and the public.). An official letter is used to communicate with other Ministries, Parastatals, and the general public.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: What should a civil servant do with a circular that demand action to be taken?
options: Rewrite the circular. □.
Read it and do nothing.
Place a copy in the relevant file and pass it to the appropriate officer for action.
Discard it.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Place a copy in the relevant file and pass it to the appropriate officer for action.). The file copy of each such circular should be placed in the file relevant to the subject matter and should be passed to the appropriate officer for action to be taken.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes considered a very serious offense for a Civil Servant?
options: Arriving late to a meeting.
Taking a day off.
Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties.
Talking to a colleague.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties.). Unauthorized disclosure of any information acquired in the course of their duties is a very serious offence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

subcategories.name: Performance & Training
subcategories.description: Performance systems, training, and capacity development.

questions.question: The primary tool used for the annual evaluation of a civil servant’s competence and performance is the:
options: Official Secrets Act Report.
Annual Performance Evaluation Report (APER/PAF).
Transfer Posting Circular.
Virement Warrant.
questions.explanation: The Annual Performance Evaluation Report (APER/PAF) is the official tool used to evaluate staff performance annually (PSR 050402).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Promotion eligibility is primarily based on which three key factors, according to the **PSR**'
options: Political affiliation, seniority, and gender.
Merit, performance, and availability of vacancy.
Personal wealth, age, and foreign training.
Attendance, handwriting, and social media activity.
questions.explanation: Promotion eligibility is based on merit, performance, and availability of vacancy, and absence of disciplinary issues (PSR 020802(d)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule defines the standard incremental date for an officer appointed or promoted to a post in the Federal Public Service
questions.explanation: The incremental date is governed by Rule 040203, which sets it as 1st January of the year following at least six months of service or promotion.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the **Performance Management Cycle** running from January to December of every year (**PSR** 050301)
options: To calculate the annual leave roster.
To ensure staff performance aligns with MDA institutional goals and targets.
To determine the final retirement age.
To manage inter-ministerial staff transfers.
questions.explanation: The Performance Management Cycle runs from January to December to align individual and institutional goals and targets.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which category of training (**PSR** 070105) is defined as a long-term, fully sponsored postgraduate study deemed **crucial** and beneficial to the officer and the **MDA** mandate
options: Category B.
Category C.
Category A.
Category D.
questions.explanation: Category A is defined as a long-term postgraduate training that is crucial and beneficial to the MDA's mandate (PSR 070105(a)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer who fails the compulsory confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts shall be required to:
options: Be promoted to the next grade level.
Resign or be withdrawn from the service.
Be interdicted on half pay.
Be transferred to a new MDA.
questions.explanation: Failure to pass the confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts requires the officer to resign or be withdrawn from the service (PSR 030104).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** permits officers to participate in courses of instruction within Nigeria, provided the course is conducted by:
options: Any private consultancy that issues attendance certificates.
Any Federal Training Centre, University or approved Public Service Training institution.
Informal apprenticeship arranged by the officer's department.
Political party training secretariats recognized by local chapters.
questions.explanation: Courses of instruction within Nigeria must be conducted by a Federal Training Centre, University, or an approved Public Service Training institution (PSR 070104).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What happens to an officer's increment if they are under interdiction or suspension (**PSR** 040202)
options: It is automatically doubled.
It shall normally be granted.
It may be withheld or deferred.
It is paid in full immediately.
questions.explanation: An increment shall normally be granted unless the officer is on interdiction/suspension or has a disciplinary action pending.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The period of deferred increment (**PSR** 040205) must not be less than three months nor more than:
options: Four months.
Six months.
Nine months.
One year.
questions.explanation: The deferment period must not be less than three months nor more than six months,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which statement regarding the restoration of a withheld increment is correct
options: It can be restored retrospectively.
It cannot be restored retrospectively.
Restoration is mandatory after three months.
Restoration is handled by the Minister of Finance.
questions.explanation: An increment deferred or withheld cannot be restored with retrospective effect in consequence of improved service during a later increment earning period (PSR 040207, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The training strategy (**PSR** 070103) aims to ensure continuous capacity development shall be encapsulated in the:
options: MDA's Annual Budget Call Circular.
Public Service Training and Capacity Development Policy.
Official Secrets Act.
Staff Nominal Roll.
questions.explanation: Strategies to ensure continuous capacity development shall be encapsulated in the Public Service Training and Capacity Development Policy,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Promotion examinations are typically required for officers on which range of Grade Levels
options: All officers upon confirmation.
questions.explanation: Promotion examinations are typically required for officers on grade levels 07 to 15 (Implied PSR policy).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: If an officer is promoted to a grade level where the new emolument point is higher than the minimum point of the new salary grade level, they shall be placed at the:
options: Minimum point of the new grade level irrespective of former emolument.
Next salary point higher than their former emolument.
Nearest lower point on the new grade to avoid pay distortion.
Mid-point of the new salary grade pending confirmation.
questions.explanation: If the emolument in the former post is higher than the minimum point of the new salary grade level, the officer will be placed on the next point, higher than their former emolument (PSR 040104(ii)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The final authority on the syllabus and guidelines for all promotion examinations rests with the:
options: Minister of Education.
Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
Budget Office of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The FCSC has the power to set guidelines for all promotions (PSR 020802(a)),,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 070101** defines staff development as the policy of enhancing the knowledge, skills, attitude, effectiveness, and efficiency of staff to meet:
options: Short-term political demands and campaign assignments.
Career goals and prepare them for changing duties and responsibilities.
Personal income targets outside official service objectives.
Union executive ambitions unrelated to organizational needs.
questions.explanation: Staff development aims to meet career goals and prepare staff for changing duties and responsibilities, Reference: Rule 070101.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For a newly appointed officer, emoluments (salary) shall generally be paid from the date of:
options: The FCSC approval.
Assumption of duty.
The letter of appointment.
Passing the initial medical test.
questions.explanation: Rule 040102 confirms that emoluments shall, as a general rule, be paid as from the date of assumption of duty.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The payment of promotion arrears to an officer (**PSR** 040104(iii)) is calculated from the:
options: Date of the new salary scale commencement.
Notional date of promotion.
Date the officer passed the promotion examination.
End of the fiscal year.
questions.explanation: Payment of promotion arrears shall be made within the year promotion is effected and calculated from the notional date of promotion.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer may be granted special leave with full pay (**PSR** 120222) to take a **non-compulsory** examination, provided the Permanent Secretary certifies that the passing of the examination is likely to:
options: Aid the officer's transfer to a foreign service.
Enhance his value to the Service.
Grant him a private business license.
Result in immediate retirement.
questions.explanation: Leave for Non-Compulsory Examination requires certification that passing the exam is likely to enhance his value to the Service (PSR 120222).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that all Officers shall be placed on the ****IPPIS** platform** within a maximum period of:
options: One week.
One month.
Two months of assumption of duty.
Six months.
questions.explanation: Rule 040102 mandates that all Officers shall be placed on the IPPIS platform within two months of assumption of duty.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body ensures promotion guidelines are followed by **MDAs** and sets definite promotion criteria
options: The Ministry of Defence.
The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The Local Government Council.
The National Youth Service Corps (NYSC).
questions.explanation: The FCSC sets definite promotion criteria and guidelines, ensuring uniformity and meritocracy.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule stipulates that an officer who fails to take the confirmation examination after 3 years of first appointment is required to resign
questions.explanation: Rule 030105 requires officers who fail to take the confirmation examination after 3 years of first appointment to resign.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer may be required to serve in **any part of the country**. This principle emphasizes:
options: Regional favoritism.
The principle of National Service.
Mandatory foreign posting.
The right to refuse posting.
questions.explanation: Officers may be required to serve in any part of the country, reflecting the principle of National Service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Training opportunities must be equitably distributed to all staff in **MDAs**, as per the objectives of:
questions.explanation: The Public Service Training and Capacity Development Policy guarantees equitable training access to all staff (PSR 070103),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a reference book allowed for use during the confirmation examination for senior officers (**GL 07** and above)
options: Public Service Rules.
Financial Regulations.
Textbooks and reference books related to the General Paper.
Civil Service Handbook.
questions.explanation: Textbooks and reference books are not allowed for the examinations in the General Paper or office Procedure/Routine or Special Paper (PSR 030107).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The authority to approve the restoration of a previously deferred increment rests with the:
options: Minister of Labour.
Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
Auditor-General of the Federation.
National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC).
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office holds the authority to decide on restoring a deferred increment (PSR 040208, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who must approve the release of any officer on study leave (**PSR** 120231)
options: The Minister of Education.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The National Assembly.
The Foreign Service Commission.
questions.explanation: The release of any Officer on study leave shall be subject to the approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (PSR 120231).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule allows for the possibility of special increments to mitigate the negative effects of a withheld increment
questions.explanation: Rule 040209 provides that the FCSC may grant one or more special increments to mitigate the effects of withholding increments (Implied rule reference).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The primary objective of the **Reward and Recognition System (R&RS)** in the civil service is to:
options: Replace the annual budgeting process.
Institutionalize a culture of excellence and motivate staff.
Simplify the process of staff dismissal.
Centralize all financial transactions.
questions.explanation: The R&RS aims to institutionalize a culture of excellence and motivate staff by recognizing meritorious service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 070210** requires a female officer who interrupts a course of training of **not more than six months duration** on grounds of pregnancy to:
options: Receive immediate promotion.
Be granted indefinite maternity leave.
Refund the whole or part of the cost of the course.
Be transferred to a non-training post.
questions.explanation: A female officer who interrupts a course of training of not more than six months duration shall be called upon to refund the whole or part of the cost of the course, Reference: Rule 070210.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who is responsible for ensuring the payment of the travel expenses, lodging, and sundries for an officer on Sabbatical Appointment
options: The Host MDA/Organization.
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The FCSC.
The National Assembly.
questions.explanation: The Host MDA/Organization shall be responsible for the payment of the travel expenses, lodging, and sundries (PSR 020414).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule ensures officers on promotion can appeal the decision if they are dissatisfied
options: Chapter 11.
questions.explanation: Aggrieved officers can petition decisions through the proper channels as described in Chapter 11 (Petitions and Appeals).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **Rule 070102** states that training is designed to benefit the individual staff and the organization by positively impacting:
options: Only staff remuneration.
Staff performance in particular and that of the organization in general.
Ministerial political campaigns.
Foreign exchange rates.
questions.explanation: Training aims to positively impact staff performance in particular and the organization in general, Reference: Rule 070102.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the maximum duration of study leave with pay that officers may be granted for postgraduate courses
options: One year.
Two years.
Three years.
Four years.
questions.explanation: Officers may be granted study leave with pay subject to a maximum of four years for postgraduate degrees (PSR 070304).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule covers the definition and objectives of staff development
questions.explanation: Staff development definition is in Rule 070101, and objectives in Rule 070102.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires the Performance Management Cycle to commence immediately after the leadership of each **MDA** has finalized their:
options: Annual leave roster.
Annual institutional and departmental goals and targets.
Annual retirement list.
Inter-ministerial posting circular.
questions.explanation: The Performance Management Cycle commences immediately after the leadership of each MDA has finalized their annual institutional and departmental goals and targets (PSR 050301).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** permits officers to make reference to specific books during the confirmation examination. These books include:
options: Only the Bible or Qu'ran.
PSR, Financial Regulations, and Civil Service Handbook.
Only legal textbooks.
Political biographies.
questions.explanation: Officers are allowed to reference the Public Service Rules, Financial Regulations, and Civil Service Handbook (PSR 030108).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule outlines the minimum number of years an officer must spend in a post before being considered eligible for promotion
questions.explanation: Rule 020802(c) outlines the minimum number of years an officer must spend in a post before being considered eligible for promotion.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Officers who are eligible to sit promotion exams while on approved **secondment or study leave** are allowed to do so to prevent:
options: Political interference.
Stagnation in their career progression.
Financial misappropriation.
Conflict of interest.
questions.explanation: Seconded officers can sit promotion exams to avoid stagnation and ensure career progression while away (PSR 020504(h)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** provides for officers to take courses of instruction under a technical assistance scheme. They receive emoluments while all other conditions of Service are decided by:
options: The FCSC.
Existing arrangements between the donor government and the Federal Government.
The officer's family lawyer.
The political party in power.
questions.explanation: Courses under a technical assistance scheme have conditions of service decided by existing arrangements between the donor government and the Federal Government (PSR 070302).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who must be notified immediately after deciding to restore an increment which had earlier been withheld or deferred
options: The Auditor-General of the Federation through the internal auditor.
The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office (to the officer).
The Federal Civil Service Commission before notifying the officer.
The Accountant-General for payroll restoration authorization.
questions.explanation: Immediately on deciding to restore an increment, the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office should bring the decision to the notice of the Officer (PSR 040208, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who is exempted from the Group A (Law) compulsory confirmation examination (**PSR** 030109)
options: Officers on GL 17 with approved departmental legal proficiency certification.
Officers with recognized law degrees from Nigerian universities or called to the Nigerian Bar.
Officers with ICT compliance certifications and completed legal orientation modules.
Officers in acting appointments who completed approved administrative law induction.
questions.explanation: Law professionals are exempted from taking Group A of the confirmation examination (PSR 030109).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires the Confirmation Examination (**PSR** 030106) to be held how frequently
options: Quarterly.
Bi-annually.
Once a year.
Every two years.
questions.explanation: Confirmation examination shall be held once a year.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the consequence if an officer leaves the Service (other than by resignation or dismissal) and enjoyed more proportionate leave than eligible (**PSR** 020905)
options: The officer loses all terminal benefits until full audit clearance.
The officer refunds salary for excess leave days already enjoyed beyond entitlement.
The officer must refund only transport and utility allowances.
The excess leave is converted automatically to leave without pay.
questions.explanation: The officer will be required to refund salary for any proportionate leave enjoyed in excess of what was eligible (PSR 020905).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 050205** mandates that the substance of any adverse comment on an officer's work or conduct must be conveyed in a report conveyed in sympathetic terms with the objective of:
options: Dismissing the officer immediately.
Enabling and encouraging him to overcome his short-comings.
Reducing the officer’s pay.
Making the report public knowledge.
questions.explanation: Adverse comments must be conveyed in sympathetic terms to encourage the officer to overcome shortcomings, Reference: Rule 050205.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Departmental Promotion Committee (DPC)** is responsible for promotion matters for officers on which grade levels
options: All cadres.
questions.explanation: The Departmental Promotion Committee handles promotion matters for officers on GL 03–06 (Implied institutional role/delegated powers).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the aim of a "handing-over note"?
options: To explain why an officer is leaving the Service.3.
To document the personal assets of an officer.
To detail the projects, files, and responsibilities of a position for a successor.
To document all the money spent by an officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To detail the projects, files, and responsibilities of a position for a successor.). The handing-over note is a detailed document that summarizes all the projects, files, and responsibilities that an officer is handing over to their successor.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: 163. How many papers must an officer on GL 09 pass to be confirmed?
options: All 6 papers of COMPRO II
All 5 papers of COMPRO I
All 5 papers of COMPRO II □
All 3 papers of COMPRO I
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (All 5 papers of COMPRO I). The syllabus in Rule 030202 lists 5 papers for COMPRO I, which officers on GL 08-09 must pass to be confirmed.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: According to the handbook, what is the proper action if papers or documents come to you by mistake from members of staff or the general public?
options: Keep them for yourself. 435
Take them to the wrong office.Q.
Find out the correct destination and take necessary directive as to re-routing it from your superior officers.
Throw them away.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Find out the correct destination and take necessary directive as to re-routing it from your superior officers.). Should papers or documents come to you by mistake from members of staff or the general public, you should find out the correct destination and take necessary directive as to re-routing it from your superior officers.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: What should intermediate officers do with papers that are routed through them for information only?
options: They should delay them unnecessarily.
They should file them immediately.3.
They should take a copy for themselves.
They should not delay them unnecessarily.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They should not delay them unnecessarily.). Intermediate officers should not delay unnecessarily papers which are routed through them for information only.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: Why is reasonable spacing used in typed drafts?
options: So that there is ample space for amendments.
To make them look more professional. □.
To make them longer.
To save paper.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (So that there is ample space for amendments.). Reasonable spacing is used in typed drafts so that there is ample space for amendments.
