questions.question: which option correctly addresses: The accountability for the conduct and supervision of these examinations for police and paramilitary services lies with whom?
options: The respective Service Commissions 52
The Federal Civil Service Commission
The Permanent Secretary of their ministry □
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The respective Service Commissions 52). The rule specifies that these examinations are "prescribed by their respective Service Commissions," implying that the commissions also conduct and supervise them.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: What does the Code of Ethics aim to achieve in the Civil Service?
options: To provide guidelines for political appointments.
To introduce new salary structures.
To reduce the number of government ministries. Q.
To continue to move the Civil Service in the right direction.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To continue to move the Civil Service in the right direction.). The handbook states that in order to continue to move the Civil Service in the right direction, the requisite Code of Ethics should be embraced by its members
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: How should the evaluation of services be based?
options: On the number of complaints received.
On objectives and programmes of activities defined beforehand, accompanied by performance indicators and criteria18.
On personal opinions.
On the amount of money spent.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (On objectives and programmes of activities defined beforehand, accompanied by performance indicators and criteria18.). The evaluation of the effectiveness and productivity of services must be based on objectives and programmes of activities defined beforehand, accompanied by performance indicators and criteria.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option correctly addresses: What emerging issues are reflected in the 2021 edition of the Public Service Rules?
options: Only financial management systems.
Only disciplinary procedures. 7
International diplomatic protocols.Q
Performance Management System, Talent Sourcing, Volunteerism, Virtual Meetings/Engagements.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Performance Management System, Talent Sourcing, Volunteerism, Virtual Meetings/Engagements.). The foreword highlights that "in the 2021 Edition of Public Service Rules (PSR), emerging issues such as performance management systems, talent sourcing, volunteerism, and virtual meetings/engagements, which are products of our recent experience in the management of the COVID-19 pandemic, and many others are reflected."
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of "interdiction"?
options: A temporary promotion.
A form of leave without payment. □
The temporary removal of an officer from performing his normal duties when disciplinary proceedings for his dismissal are being undertaken.
A permanent dismissal from service.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The temporary removal of an officer from performing his normal duties when disciplinary proceedings for his dismissal are being undertaken.). Rule 010105 defines "interdiction" as "the temporary removal of an officer from performing his normal duties when disciplinary proceedings for his dismissal are being undertaken or is about to be undertaken, and he must be placed on half salary pending the determination of the case."
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, which option aligns with the meaning of the consequence for a public officer found to be a member of a secret society?
options: A written warning 19
Demotion Q
Serious misconduct leading to dismissal
Immediate suspension
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Serious misconduct leading to dismissal). Rule 020212 states that contravention of Rule 020211 (Prohibition of Membership of Secret Society) "must be regarded as an act of serious misconduct which may include dismissal from the service." Part 5: Rules for Appointment on Probation
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: An officer may be dismissed from service for what reason?
options: Gross misconduct
Leaving the service a
Resignation
Termination
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Gross misconduct). Rule 020903 defines "termination" but does not explicitly mention "dismissal" in the provided text. However, Rule 100407 defines dismissal and its effects. This question is a knowledge gap in the provided text. Rule 020212 mentions that an act of serious misconduct may include dismissal from the service. Rule 100401 defines gross misconduct. Therefore, the most appropriate answer is "Gross misconduct."
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, select the statement that correctly defines the consequence if an officer negligently disregards the provisions of Regulation 719 when accepting a cheque that is subsequently dishonored?
options: Suspension of duties.
A formal warning. 215
Surcharge for the full amount.
Referral to the Auditor-General.Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Surcharge for the full amount.). Financial Regulation 731 states: "An officer accepting a cheque which is subsequently dishonoured must be surcharged for the full amount, if the provisions Of Regulation 719 were negligently disregarded."
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes one of the main objectives of the Due workflow policy?
options: To increase government spending on all projects
To strictly enforce due procedure principles of transparency, competition, and efficiency
To centralize all procurement decisions at the federal level a.
To promote extra-budgetary spending in Ministries, Departments, and Agencies (MDAs)
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To strictly enforce due procedure principles of transparency, competition, and efficiency). A main objective is to strictly enforce due workflow principles of transparency, competition, efficiency, and value for money in the procurement of public goods, works, and services.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, select the statement that correctly defines the objective of the single internet portal to be established by the Bureau of Public Procurement?
options: To facilitate international tenders exclusively3.
To be a platform for public complaints only
To serve as a principal and definitive source of all information on government procurement
To serve as a private communication channel for government officials
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To serve as a principal and definitive source of all information on government procurement). The Bureau is to establish a single internet portal that will serve as a chief and 388 definitive source of all information on government procurement.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: The Administrative and Professional Officers' duties are derived from what?
options: The needs of the public.Q.
The system of Government.
The financial regulations.
The directives of the Permanent Secretary.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The system of Government.). The raison d'etre and duties of the Administrative and the Professional Officers derive from our system of Government.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which role has the constitutional power to appoint persons to offices in the Federal Civil Service and exercise disciplinary control over them?
options: The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The National Council on Establishments (NCE).
The President. □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).). The FCSC is the constitutional body that has the power to appoint persons to offices in the Federal Civil Service and exercise disciplinary control over them.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: select the statement that correctly defines a duty of the Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC)?
options: Managing the day-to-day operations of Ministries.
Approving the budget of MDAs. Q.
Serving as an appellate body for petitions from officers who feel aggrieved on appointment, promotion, and discipline matters.
Coordinating service-wide training.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Serving as an appellate body for petitions from officers who feel aggrieved on appointment, promotion, and discipline matters.). The FCSC serves as an appellate body in respect of petitions from Officers in the MDAs who feel aggrieved concerning appointment, promotion, and discipline matters.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: What should a Civil Servant do if they detect financial abuses?
options: Report them to a friend or colleague.
Wait for a superior officer to detect it. Q.
Report them through the correct channel.
Ignore them, as it is not their duty.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Report them through the correct channel.). As a good citizen and a good civil servant, one should discourage financial abuses by making suitable reports through the correct channel.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: The handbook lists a number of attributes that Civil Servants should possess to guarantee quality service delivery. Which of the following is NOT one of them?
options: Be punctual and dedicated.
Be willing to accept bribes.
Be efficient and loyal. □.
Be polite and cheerful.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Be willing to accept bribes.). The handbook lists a number of attributes for Civil Servants to secure quality service delivery, including being punctual, polite, cheerful, efficient, and loyal. Accepting bribes is a corrupt act that is discouraged..
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: According to the Civil Service Handbook, what is a main element in good governance?
options: Policy formulationa.
Political appointments
Service delivery
Financial management
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Service delivery). The handbook states that service delivery is a chief element in good governance, and efficient and effective delivery of good service is the raison d'etre of government.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: choose the best answer for: What should a Civil Servant do with a file when they are finished with it?
options: Keep it in their desk for future reference.
Pass it to a colleague without informing them.a.
Throw it away if it is not needed anymore.
Put it on a "bring-up" pad if action is necessitate at a later date.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Put it on a "bring-up" pad if action is necessitate at a later date.). When an officer has finished with a file, he should secure that it is returned to the registry. If a file is demand at a later date, it should be put on a "bring-up" pad.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the aim of a "handing-over note"?
options: To detail the projects, files, and responsibilities of a position for a successor.
To document all the money spent by an officer.
To explain why an officer is leaving the Service.3.
To document the personal assets of an officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To detail the projects, files, and responsibilities of a position for a successor.). (To detail the projects, files, and responsibilities of a position for a successor.). The handing-over note is a detailed document that summarizes all the projects, files, and responsibilities that an officer is handing over to their successor.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: How should a Civil Servant handle a file that has been left on their desk and has been forgotten?
options: Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.
Throw it away.
Keep it in their desk.
Read the contents to see if it is important.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Report it to their superior officer and return it to the registry.). Should a file be left on a desk and has been forgotten, it should be reported to the superior officer and returned to the registry.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

subcategories.name: Administrative Procedures
subcategories.description: Office procedure, communication, and records workflow.

questions.question: The administrative structure that groups officers by professional discipline (e.g., Admin, Finance, Legal) is known as the:
options: Grade Levels (GL).
Scheme of Service (or Cadre).
Annual Budget Head.
IPPIS Platform.
questions.explanation: The Scheme of Service (Cadre) is the administrative structure that groups officers by professional discipline,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary function of the **Registry Unit** in an **MDA**'
options: Maintaining budget ledgers and commitment control registers.
Managing records, correspondence, file movement, and document tracking.
Conducting staff promotion interviews and confirmation examinations.
Auditing imprest retirements and sanctioning financial defaults.
questions.explanation: The Registry Unit is responsible for the file movement, correspondence handling, and documentation tracking (Implied administrative function).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that official communication must generally pass through:
options: Social media channels.
Directly to the Minister.
Established hierarchical channels.
The officer's residence.
questions.explanation: All official communications must follow established hierarchical channels for proper documentation and accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The primary purpose of stressing **proper handover** during transfers is to ensure:
options: Personal discretion is maintained.
Continuity of duties and accountability in civil service operations.
The transfer is delayed indefinitely.
The incoming officer is immediately promoted.
questions.explanation: Proper handover ensures continuity of duties and accountability in civil service operations.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: All official documents must be treated as the property of whom
options: The officer who generated them.
The Head of Department.
The Government.
The Public (under the FOI Act).
questions.explanation: All official documents are government property, not the personal property of officers.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The responsibility for coordinating service-wide reforms and issuing Establishment Circulars rests primarily with the:
options: Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
Ministry of Finance.
The Presidency's media team.
questions.explanation: The OHCSF issues Establishment Circulars and coordinates service reforms.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The authority to promote officers on **GL 06** and its equivalent or below is primarily vested in the:
options: Ministerial Tenders Board (MTB).
Permanent Secretary/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices.
Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The Governor of the Central Bank.
questions.explanation: Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices are authorized to promote eligible candidates to posts up to GL 06 (PSR 020801(b)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Senior Staff Committee (SSC)** handles promotion and disciplinary matters for officers on which grade levels in Parastatals
options: GL 07 and above.
Only GL 17.
Officers on contract appointment only.
questions.explanation: The Senior Staff Committee handles decisions for officers on GL 07 and above (PSR 170301).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The statutory minimum number of years an officer must spend in a post before being considered eligible for promotion is determined by:
options: Ministerial discretion.
The officer’s personal request.
The National Assembly.
questions.explanation: Rule 020802(c) outlines the minimum number of years an officer must spend in a post before being considered eligible for promotion.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The cost of entitlements due to an employee whose **posting requires a change of duty station** shall be borne by:
options: The employee personally.
The receiving MDA.
The posting MDA.
The Federal Ministry of Finance.
questions.explanation: The cost of entitlements due to the employee whose posting requires a change of duty station shall be borne by the posting MDA (PSR 020607).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that any complaints or grievances with respect to posting instructions should be submitted through the appropriate channel within:
options: One week.
Three working days.
Seven working days.
One month.
questions.explanation: Complaints/grievances must be submitted through the appropriate channel within three working days (PSR 020606).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule governs the remuneration for retired officers re-engaged on contract appointment
questions.explanation: Rule 020412 covers retired officers re-engaged into career posts; remuneration shall be based on negotiable terms.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer on **secondment** to another Government or international organization may retain their substantive post and be entitled to increment and promotion, provided the period of secondment shall not exceed:
options: Three years.
Four years.
Six years.
Ten years.
questions.explanation: During the period of secondment, the period shall not exceed six years (PSR 020504(h)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** permits an officer to appeal a disciplinary decision through the proper channels as described in:
options: Chapter 5 (Performance).
Chapter 11 (Petitions and Appeals).
Chapter 12 (Leave).
Chapter 15 (Ethics).
questions.explanation: Aggrieved officers can petition decisions through the proper channels as described in Chapter 11 (Petitions and Appeals).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the mandatory retirement age for all grades in the Service
options: 55 years of age or 30 years of service, whichever is earlier.
60 years of age or 35 years of pensionable service, whichever is earlier.
65 years of age for all cadres regardless of years of service.
62 years of age or 35 years of pensionable service, whichever is earlier.
questions.explanation: The mandatory retirement age is 60 years or 35 years of pensionable service, whichever is earlier (Implied PSR rule).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires the appointment, promotion, and discipline of senior staff in Federal Government Parastatals to be handled by the:
options: Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
Boards/Councils through the appropriate Staff Management Committees.
Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The President directly.
questions.explanation: All appointments, promotions, and discipline for senior and junior officers in Parastatals shall be made on the authority of the Boards/Councils (PSR 170301).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** defines **Acting Appointment** (**PSR** 020703) as being **NOT** intended as a means of testing the suitability of Officers for promotion, but normally made only in order to fill posts that are:
options: Substantive and pensionable, with immediate confirmation after six months.
Temporarily vacant, with acting duration limited to the period of vacancy.
Newly created and permanently assigned to junior officers in the cadre.
Under litigation and therefore excluded from supervisory functions.
questions.explanation: Acting appointment is not a trial for promotion and is used only to fill temporarily vacant posts for a limited duration.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** 020704 states that the period of acting appointment shall **not** exceed:
options: One month.
Six months.
One year.
Two years.
questions.explanation: Recommendations for acting appointment shall not exceed a period of one year.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which document is used to update or supplement the Public Service Rules (**PSR**)
options: Judgments from the Supreme Court.
Circulars issued by the Head of Service or Presidency.
Annual Budget Call Circulars.
Trade Union Constitutions.
questions.explanation: The PSR defines Circulars as supplementary directives (Implied rule reference).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** **Rule 020507** outlines conditions for an applicant to be considered for:
options: Dismissal.
Sabbatical leave.
Transfer.
Promotion to GL 17.
questions.explanation: Rule 020507 outlines conditions for transfer, ensuring vacancies exist and the move does not jeopardize promotion prospects.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An appeal/petition will **NOT** be entertained if it is submitted more than six months after the decision complained of has been given, unless:
options: It is supported by political figures.
The appeal is supported by valid reasons for the delay.
The officer pays a fine.
The officer is under interdiction.
questions.explanation: An appeal/petition submitted more than six months late will not be entertained unless such delay is supported by valid reasons (PSR 110209(b)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The remuneration of Non-Public/Civil Servants re-engaged after retirement (**PSR** 020412) shall be based on:
options: A fixed amount set by the FCSC.
Negotiable terms by the parties concerned.
The officer's last paid salary.
The minimum wage only.
questions.explanation: The remuneration of Non-Public/Civil Servants re-engaged shall be based on negotiable terms by the parties concerned.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that petitions concerning administrative decisions affecting an officer personally must generally be handled within a maximum period of:
options: 30 days.
Three months.
Six months.
One year.
questions.explanation: Appeals must generally be handled expeditiously, with six months often serving as the general administrative limit (implied by 110209(b) delay clause).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When an Officer is seconded to another Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office, who is responsible for furnishing the performance reports (**PSR** 050206)
options: The parent Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office.
The receiving Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office to which he is seconded.
The FCSC.
The Auditor-General of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The receiving Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office to which the officer is seconded is responsible for furnishing the performance reports.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The use of **Delegation of Authority** (CSH) primarily aims to improve efficiency while ensuring:
options: Responsibility is transferred entirely to subordinates.
Ultimate responsibility remains with the senior delegating officer.
Accountability is reduced.
The process is kept secret.
questions.explanation: Delegation improves efficiency by allowing junior officers to act, but ultimate responsibility remains with the senior officer.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Junior Staff Committee (JSC)** handles all appointments, promotions, and discipline matters for officers on which grade levels
options: GL 15 and above.
All officers on probation.
questions.explanation: The Junior Staff Committee handles appointments and discipline of officers on GL 01–06 (PSR 170301).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule covers the conditions for an officer to be considered for transfer-on-promotion
questions.explanation: No applicant shall be considered for transfer-on-promotion to any MDA except in response to an advertised post (PSR 020506).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the consequence if a petition repeats the substance of a previous petition without introducing new relevant matter (**PSR** 110208(v))
options: The petition must be forwarded to the FCSC.
The appeal/petition will not be entertained.
The officer is automatically suspended.
The appeal is automatically approved.
questions.explanation: An appeal/petition will not be entertained if it merely repeats the substance of a previous petition without introducing new relevant matter.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that Trade Unions/Staff Associations introduce a tenure limit of two (2) terms, which shall not be more than:
options: Two years.
Three years.
Four years.
Six years.
questions.explanation: The duration for each tenure shall not be more than 4 years (PSR 020612).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The maximum period for which an officer on probation can fail to secure confirmation in the pensionable establishment before being terminated is:
options: One year (initial period).
Two years (initial period).
Three years (including extension).
Four years (total service time).
questions.explanation: The PSR sets an initial two-year probation period (PSR 020301) before confirmation, with termination possible if confirmation fails after extensions prescribed.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** states that an officer on secondment shall be appraised by:
options: The parent MDA only.
The MDA he is seconded to.
The FCSC.
The Auditor-General of the Federation.
questions.explanation: An Officer on secondment shall be appraised by the MDA he is seconded to (PSR 050206),,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The primary component of government administration that deals with personnel and staffing issues (appointments, promotion, discipline) is referred to as:
options: Procurement.
Financial Management.
Establishment Matters.
Foreign Policy.
questions.explanation: Establishment matters concern appointments, promotion, and discipline (personnel and staffing issues).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body must approve the Conditions of Service, Scheme of Service, and Organizational Structure for Parastatals
options: The Minister of Justice.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The Federal Ministry of Education.
The National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC).
questions.explanation: Conditions of Service, Scheme of Service and Organizational Structure for Parastatals shall be approved by the OHCSF (PSR 170103).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In parastatals, which level of authority does a Minister exercise control
options: Directly in the day-to-day management.
Policy level only through the Board/Council of the Parastatal.
Staff recruitment below GL 07.
Financial transactions only.
questions.explanation: A Minister exercises control of Parastatals at the policy level only through the Board of the Parastatal (PSR 170201).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that where an Officer whose spouse lives and works in a different location wishes to reside in that location, the Government shall:
options: Decline the request and direct the officer to remain at official posting location.
Bear the relocation cost where posting is at the officer's request under the applicable rule.
Approve relocation only after converting the officer's post to a lower grade.
Transfer the spouse into federal service before granting residence approval.
questions.explanation: The Government shall be responsible for the cost of relocating the Officer whose posting is at his request, subject to certain conditions (PSR 020608).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 020606** allows officers to seek redress where they are of the opinion that their job/location movement has not been done in line with:
options: Their personal preference.
The provisions of extant rules.
The Minister's verbal promise.
Trade union instructions.
questions.explanation: Officers may seek redress where they believe movement was not done in line with the provisions of extant rules, Reference: Rule 020606.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who is responsible for identifying, initiating, and disseminating information for volunteer programs and internship opportunities (**PSR** 020417)
options: The FCSC.
The Focal Office established in the OHCSF.
The National Youth Service Corps (NYSC).
The Minister of Labour.
questions.explanation: A Focal Office established in the OHCSF is responsible for identifying, initiating, and disseminating information for volunteer and internship programs.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that where an officer is seconded or posted, all non-salary allowances shall be paid by:
options: The receiving MDA.
The parent MDA.
The officer personally.
The FCSC.
questions.explanation: All non-salary allowances shall be paid by receiving MDAs (PSR 020504).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** definition of an **Internship** (**PSR** 020415) relates to structured experience that helps to enhance:
options: Political campaigning skills.
Academic, career, professional and personal development.
Personal financial assets.
Ability to circumvent rules.
questions.explanation: Internship helps to enhance academic, career, professional and personal development, and facilitates acquisition of practical skills.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule ensures that an appeal/petition is not entertained if it is illegible or meaningless
options: Rule 110208(iii).
questions.explanation: An appeal/petition will not be entertained if it is illegible or meaningless (PSR 110208(iii)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires Ministries to notify the **FCSC** when officers revert from acting appointment by completion of Form No. Gen. 15A which shall be forwarded not less than:
options: Three working days before the officer stops performing acting duties.
Two weeks before cessation of the acting appointment.
At the end of the quarter in which the acting appointment terminates.
Only after cessation has taken effect and handover is completed.
questions.explanation: The notification of cessation of acting appointment must be forwarded not less than two weeks before cessation (PSR 020707).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule stipulates that the emoluments and incremental date of an officer on transfer is governed by **Rule 040103**'
questions.explanation: Rule 020505 confirms that the emoluments and incremental date of an Officer on transfer are governed by Rule 040103.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** generally states that all previous editions of the **PSR** are:
options: Fully valid for ongoing disciplinary cases.
Entirely repealed and replaced, with savings for ongoing cases.
Used to interpret ambiguities in new rules.
Applicable only to pre-2021 appointees.
questions.explanation: The PSR 2021 repeals all previous editions but contains savings provisions for cases already in progress.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Meritocracy** in the civil service requires that appointments into all grades in Parastatals must be based on:
options: Political patronage balanced with internal seniority ranking.
Service need, merit, credibility, and equal employment opportunity.
Regional representation quota as the overriding selection factor.
Length of service in the parastatal regardless of competence profile.
questions.explanation: Appointments into all grades in Parastatals shall be need-based, ensuring merit, credibility and equal employment opportunity (PSR 170302).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In disciplinary matters, which misconduct is classified as **Gross Misconduct** according to the Civil Service Handbook
options: Habitual lateness.
Failure to sign attendance register.
Fraud and falsification of records.
Poor communication.
questions.explanation: Fraud and falsification of records constitute gross misconduct Reference: CSH Discipline.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which chapter of the Civil Service Handbook provides grievance procedures to resolve disputes within the civil service
options: Chapter 3 (Discipline).
Chapter 4 (Accountability).
Chapter 7 (Grievance).
Chapter 9 (Collaboration).
questions.explanation: Chapter 7 provides grievance procedures to resolve disputes within the civil service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a core value of civil servants, according to the Civil Service Handbook
options: Secrecy.
Efficiency.
Loyalty.
Favoritism.
questions.explanation: The handbook emphasizes secrecy, efficiency, and loyalty as core values Reference: CSH Ethics.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Administrative Procedures, which action best demonstrates administrative procedures governance
options: Apply approved administrative procedures procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved administrative procedures procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective administrative procedures, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure administrative procedures compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on administrative procedures risk management within Administrative Procedures
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling administrative procedures, which choice reflects proper administrative ethics standards
options: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain grievance handling in Administrative Procedures
options: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Administrative Procedures, which action best demonstrates performance management
options: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective administrative procedures, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure discipline and conduct
options: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on anti-corruption safeguards within Administrative Procedures
options: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling administrative procedures, which choice reflects proper documented procedure standards
options: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain compliance assurance in Administrative Procedures
options: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Apply approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Administrative Procedures, which action best demonstrates public accountability
options: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Provide traceable decisions and evidence-based justification. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective administrative procedures, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on risk control within Administrative Procedures
options: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify risk early, apply controls, and document mitigation. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling administrative procedures, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in administrative procedures Reference: Administrative Procedures - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain operational discipline in Administrative Procedures
options: Follow approved workflows and verify outputs before closure.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes one of the Accountant-General's duties regarding compliance with rules and regulations?
options: To manage all federal government investments.3
To issue warnings to defaulting officers.
To approve all government contracts.
To conduct routine and in-depth inspection of books of accounts to guarantee compliance.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To conduct routine and in-depth inspection of books of accounts to guarantee compliance.). Financial Regulation 107(h) states the Accountant-General must "conduct routine and in-depth inspection of the books of accounts of federal ministries... to guarantee compliance with rules, regulations, and policy decisions."
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: Question: What is one of the Accountant-General's duties related to revenue?
options: To carry out revenue monitoring and accounting.
To approve all revenue expenditures. □
To set revenue targets for ministries.
To collect all federal government revenue.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (To carry out revenue monitoring and accounting.). Financial Regulation 107(m) states the Accountant-General must "carry out revenue monitoring and accounting."
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of one of the duties of the Head of Finance and Accounts of a ministry?
options: To conduct external audits of the ministry.Q
To directly disburse all public funds.
To approve all government contracts. 152
To guarantee compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by staff.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To guarantee compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by staff.). Financial Regulation 114(a) states the Head of Finance and Accounts is in charge for "ensuring compliance with Financial Regulations and the Accounting Code by all staff under his control and supervision."

options: The operations of non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
Private sector investments
Foreign direct investmentss.
Costing of projects and services
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Costing of projects and services). The Due workflow is aimed at ensuring fiscal transparency and strict compliance 20 with procedures, effectiveness, and efficiency in the costing of projects and services.

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: A key challenge related to the recruitment workflow in the Civil Service is the perception that:?

It is too transparent and equitable.
The Federal Character principle is being applied correctly.Q.
User agencies play dominant roles in the recruitment of senior officers.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The recruitment workflow and advancement procedure need to be reviewed to guarantee equity 417 and transparency.). There is a perception that both the recruitment procedure and the advancement procedure need to be reviewed to guarantee equity and transparency.

questions.question: According to established rules, which option best describes the duty of every officer regarding the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars?
options: To ignore them if inconvenient.
To challenge them if they disagree
To acquaint himself with them.
To delegate the accountability to subordinates.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To acquaint himself with them.). Rule 010103 states, "It must be the duty of every officer to acquaint himself with the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars."

questions.question: In the public service context, which time frame is a Revenue Collector not relieved of their duty by delegation?
options: When the person delegated to is also a Revenue Collector
According to Financial Regulation 125, the delegation of duty does not relieve the officer of their pecuniary responsibilities.
Only if the delegation is in writing.
Never, they are always accountable.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (According to Financial Regulation 125, the delegation of duty does not relieve the officer of their pecuniary responsibilities.). Financial Regulation 125 states that a delegation of duty to a subordinate officer does not relieve the delegating officer of their pecuniary duty, which applies to Revenue Collectors.

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes one of the duties of the Accountant-General regarding accounting codes and manuals?
options: To distribute them to the public. □
To formulate them without external input.
To only approve them.
To approve and secure compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock verification manuals.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To approve and secure compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock verification manuals.). Financial Regulation 107(i) states: "approve and guarantee compliance with accounting codes, internal audit guides and stock verification manuals of federal ministries, extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government."

questions.question: In official practice, which option best describes the procedure for deposits held in foreign currency?
options: They must be converted to local currency immediately.

They are the duty of the Central Bank of Nigeria. □
They should be treated in the same manner as deposits in local currency.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They should be treated in the same manner as deposits in local currency.). While not explicitly stated, the general principle of the financial regulations is that all government funds are subject to proper accounting procedures, regardless of the currency. The regulations for deposits apply universally.

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: What happens if an officer fails to secure the "full repayment" of an advance, as per Financial Regulation 1419?

The officer is granted an extensiona

The outstanding balance is transferred to another department
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Disciplinary action may be taken). While not explicitly stated in the provided snippet for FR 1419, failure to comply with financial regulations generally leads to disciplinary action.

They may face disciplinary action and surcharges.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They may face disciplinary action and surcharges.). The emphasis on "strict observance" 26 implies consequences for failure.

Call the attention of the appropriate officer in writing.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Call the attention of the appropriate officer in writing.). Financial Regulation 1703(a) states the Internal Auditor must call the attention of the appropriate officer in writing where there is any apparent abuse of Financial Regulations.
