questions.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: What does the "Transparency" characteristic of good governance imply regarding information?
options: Information is freely available and accessible to those who will be affected by decisions.
Information is only shared with government officials.
Information is strictly controlled and limited.
Decisions are taken without explaining the reasons
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Information is freely available and accessible to those who will be affected by decisions.). Under Transparency, information is freely available and accessible to those who will be affected by such decisions and their enforcement.
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: What should adjustments to the way services are organized and operated lead to?
options: More bureaucracy.
Increased costs.a.
Better service delivery.
Fewer services for the public.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Better service delivery.). Adjustments to the way in which services are organized and operated must lead to better service delivery and must be informed by best practices in the application of the information and communication technologies.
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: select the option that best answers: How should the deadlines for service delivery be determined?
options: In a realistic manner and must be communicated to users27.
In an arbitrary manner.
By the whims of the head of department.
By the political party in power.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (In a realistic manner and must be communicated to users27.). Such deadlines must be determined in a realistic manner, and must be communicated to users of public services.
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: A key principle of service delivery is to be responsive. What does this mean?
options: Only serving those who ask for help.
Only serving those who are powerful.
Delaying services as much as possible. □.
Serving all stakeholders within a reasonable timeframe.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Serving all stakeholders within a reasonable timeframe.). The principle of Responsiveness states that institutions and processes try to serve all stakeholders within a reasonable timeframe.
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the statement that correctly defines the chief accountability of all employees of the Federal Government with regard to union activities?
options: To secure the union's executives are posted to suitable locations.
To discharge the duties assigned by their employer to the Nigerian public in a timely and efficient manner.
To participate in all union meetings.
To report to the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation. □
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To discharge the duties assigned by their employer to the Nigerian public in a timely and efficient manner.). Rule 020609 states that the chief duty of all employees is "to discharge the duties assigned by their employer to the Nigerian public in a timely and efficient manner."
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: responsibility for has been assigned the duty for all financial business of the government of the federation by the President belongs to which role?
options: The Auditor-General for the Federation.
The Accountant-General of the Federation.
The Minister of Finance. Q
The Head of the Civil Service
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Minister of Finance. Q). Financial Regulation 103 states, "The President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria has assigned to the Minister of Finance the accountability for all financial business of the government of the federation." 145
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option best describes the accountability of the members of the Board of Survey regarding the report?
options: They are only accountable for the physical counting
Only the Chairman is accountable for the report's accuracy.
They are jointly accountable for the accuracy of the report.
They are not accountable for the contents of the report.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (They are jointly accountable for the accuracy of the report.). The fact that the report must be signed by all members implies that they are jointly in charge for its accuracy.
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: choose the best answer for: The current integrated structure of the Federal Civil Service is likened to what shape?
options: A pyramid.
A straight line. □.
A circle.
A square.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (A pyramid.). The integrated structure which exists in the Federal Civil Service as of today is like a pyramid.
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: which option best describes the objective of the National Council on Establishments (NCE), which has been in existence since 1957? 418?
options: For experience sharing and harmonization of human resource issues between Federal and State Governments.
To manage the careers of all civil servants.
To recruit all new employees into the civil service.a.
To handle all disciplinary cases for federal staff.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (For experience sharing and harmonization of human resource issues between Federal and State Governments.). The National Council on Establishments has been in existence since 1957 for experience sharing, alignment, and harmonization of human resource issues between the Federal and the State Governments.
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the danger of a Civil Servant accepting gifts from contractors and business people?
options: It could compromise the integrity of the Civil Servant.
It is an act of kindness.
It is a normal part of business. □.
It will create a good relationship.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (It could compromise the integrity of the Civil Servant.). (It could compromise the integrity of the Civil Servant.). The Civil Servant should remember that the acceptance of gifts from contractors and business people could compromise his position and his integrity
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option best describes the implication of failing to take reasonable care of documents entrusted to a Civil Servant?
options: It is an offence.
It is a minor infraction.
It is a normal part of the job.Q.
It is an excusable mistake.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (It is an offence.). It is an offence for a Civil Servant to fail to take reasonable care of any documents entrusted to his care.
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the option that best answers: The handbook advises Civil Servants to avoid what when dealing with the public?
options: Attending to their requests promptly.
Helping them when possible.
Asking for gifts or favors. Q. 432
Being helpful, but still being impartial.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Asking for gifts or favors. Q. 432). It is a disgrace for a Civil Servant to tout for official matters and demand money or gifts from members of the public.
questions.chapter: Innovation & Technology in Service - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

subcategories.name: Service Delivery & Grievance
subcategories.description: Service standards, user experience, and grievance handling.

questions.question: Which chapter of the Civil Service Handbook emphasizes **service delivery** as the core of civil service responsibilities
options: Chapter 1.
Chapter 4.
Chapter 6.
Chapter 9.
questions.explanation: Chapter 4 emphasizes service delivery as the key role of civil servants in meeting citizens' needs.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The public service initiative focused on enhancing service quality, timeliness, and customer satisfaction is known as:
options: IPPIS.
Servicom.
TSA.
GIFMIS.
questions.explanation: Servicom (Service Compact with All Nigerians) pledges public service efficiency and citizen-centered delivery.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which section of the Civil Service Handbook outlines grievance procedures to resolve disputes within the civil service
options: Chapter 3.
Chapter 5.
Chapter 7.
Chapter 9.
questions.explanation: Chapter 7 provides grievance procedures to resolve disputes within the civil service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An aggrieved officer who wishes to appeal a disciplinary decision must generally submit their appeal within how many days of the decision being communicated
options: 7 days.
14 days.
30 days.
60 days.
questions.explanation: Appeals must generally be submitted within 30 days of the decision being communicated.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Appeals against promotion bypass should be heard within:
options: 30 days.
60 days.
90 days.
120 days.
questions.explanation: Promotion bypass appeals should be heard within 30 days.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Impartiality** in service delivery guarantees:
options: Service is provided only to government officials.
Fair and unbiased service to all citizens.
Priority access to political figures.
Delayed service implementation.
questions.explanation: Impartiality guarantees that civil servants serve all citizens fairly without political or personal bias.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Freedom of Information (**FOI**) Act** supports public service transparency by ensuring:
options: All staff must declare assets annually.
The public has the right to access government-held information.
Promotion records are always secret.
Contracts are awarded through direct procurement only.
questions.explanation: The FOI Act grants the public the right to request and access public records.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body typically handles petitions regarding allowances or entitlements (**PSR** practice)
options: The Auditor-General.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS).
The Minister of Justice.
questions.explanation: The OHCSF handles petitions concerning allowances and similar entitlements.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which principle in the Handbook mandates that decisions and processes are open, with records available for accountability
options: Secrecy.
Transparency.
Anonymity.
Neutrality.
questions.explanation: Transparency requires that decisions and processes are open, with records available for accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer may seek redress for grievance regarding posting instructions (**Rule 020606**) if they believe the decision violates:
options: The opinion of their family.
The provisions of extant rules.
The current market interest rate.
The foreign exchange policy.
questions.explanation: Officers may seek redress where they believe movement was not done in line with the provisions of extant rules Reference: Rule 020606).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to the Civil Service Handbook, respecting **diversity** in the workplace is important to:
options: Reduce efficiency.
Foster inclusivity and unity.
Promote nepotism.
Simplify disciplinary action.
questions.explanation: Respecting diversity fosters inclusivity, unity, and fairness in the workplace.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Efficiency** in service delivery requires that government services:
options: Are executed slowly and deliberately.
Meet public needs effectively and deliver value for money.
Are provided only to the highest bidder.
Require constant external consultant input.
questions.explanation: Efficiency ensures civil servants meet public needs effectively and deliver value for money.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** permits **only one petition for review** per disciplinary matter (**Rule 120230**). This is intended to prevent:
options: Quick administrative closure.
Frivolous and unending appeals.
Legal recourse to the courts.
The issuance of a written query.
questions.explanation: Only one petition for review is allowed per matter to prevent frivolous and unending appeals Reference: Rule 120230).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The ethical requirement for accountability in the use of government resources is emphasized in which chapter of the Handbook
options: Chapter 1.
Chapter 2.
Chapter 4.
Chapter 5.
questions.explanation: Chapter 4 emphasizes accountability in the utilization of government funds and resources.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: If an appeal against a disciplinary decision is pending in a Court of Law, the administrative appeal/petition will:
options: Be automatically approved.
Be suspended until the court case is decided.
Be transferred to the FCSC.
Not be entertained (Rule 110208(ii)).
questions.explanation: An appeal/petition will not be entertained if it deals with a case in which legal action is pending in a Court of Law (Rule 110208(ii)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The final conclusion of an appeal/petition (**Rule 110210**) must state concisely:
options: The officer's cadre history and previous deployment records.
The nature of the redress sought and a summary of the reasons adduced in support of the redress sought.
The names and signatures of all committee members present.
The MDA's annual budget vote and implementation status.
questions.explanation: The conclusion of an appeal/petition shall state concisely the nature of the redress sought and a summary of the reasons adduced in support of the redress sought Reference: Rule 110210).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the **FCSC** overseeing disciplinary procedures (**Rule 100305**)
options: To ensure the officer is dismissed quickly.
To ensure the process upholds due process and natural justice.
To transfer all officers to other MDAs.
To negotiate a high pension payout.
questions.explanation: The FCSC’s oversight ensures that the disciplinary process adheres to due process and principles of natural justice Reference: Rule 100305).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Anonymity** supports service delivery by directing public attention and accountability towards:
options: The political executive (Minister/President).
The senior civil servant providing the advice.
The Permanent Secretary.
The junior staff.
questions.explanation: Anonymity ensures that civil servants do not seek recognition, directing praise or blame for policy outcomes toward the political executive.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** encourages civil servants to maintain high ethical standards, which directly correlates with the delivery of:
options: Biased and political service.
Impartial and incorruptible service.
Delayed administrative processes.
High overhead expenditure.
questions.explanation: High ethical standards promote impartial and incorruptible service, enhancing public trust.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Petitions regarding disciplinary matters must be submitted through the proper channel within:
options: Three working days (implied for grievances/complaints).
30 calendar days of the decision.
Six months.
One year.
questions.explanation: Appeals must generally be submitted within 30 days of the decision being communicated, following the time limits for appeals.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body serves as an advocate for the strict compliance with due process and operational rules in the service
options: The FCSC (implied role in professionalism).
The National Assembly.
The National Council on Public Procurement.
The Ministry of Works.
questions.explanation: The FCSC serves as an advocate for the strict compliance with due process and operational rules in the service, and promotes the professionalization of the Service Reference: rules.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Meritocracy** in the civil service is a key component of service delivery excellence because it ensures:
options: Appointments are based primarily on years served in the cadre.
The most competent and qualified individuals are responsible for public service delivery.
Political loyalty is prioritized where service urgency is declared.
Regional balancing overrides technical competence requirements.
questions.explanation: Meritocracy ensures that appointments and promotions are based on competence, leading to higher quality service delivery.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** guarantees that a petition will **NOT** be entertained if it is worded in:
options: Formal administrative language and respectful presentation.
Abusive, improper, or foul language (Rule 110208(iv)).
Technical language supported by documented references.
Structured memo format submitted through due channel.
questions.explanation: An appeal/petition will not be entertained if it is worded in abusive, improper, or foul language.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Rewards and Recognition System (R&RS)** is a service delivery tool designed to:
options: Punish underperforming staff.
Incentivize high performance and professional excellence.
Reduce staff salaries.
Promote nepotism.
questions.explanation: R&RS incentivizes high performance and professional excellence, driving improved service delivery.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 020606** allows officers to seek redress for grievances regarding posting instructions. This is handled through:
options: Public protest.
Submission through the appropriate channel.
Directly contacting the President.
A lawsuit without internal redress.
questions.explanation: Complaints/grievances must be submitted through the appropriate channel Reference: Rule 020606.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which principle guarantees that citizens receive fair and equitable service, regardless of their ethnic, religious, or political background
options: Loyalty.
Neutrality/Impartiality.
Secrecy.
Anonymity.
questions.explanation: Neutrality/Impartiality ensures service is fair and equitable to all citizens.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** mandates that all officers must be familiar with and comply strictly with the **PSR**. This rule aims to ensure:
options: Ignorance is an excuse for failure.
Uniformity and compliance in administrative and disciplinary processes.
Officers delay official duties.
Only senior officers understand the rules.
questions.explanation: Officers must familiarize themselves and comply strictly with the PSR; ignorance is not an excuse.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** Chapter allows an aggrieved officer to petition decisions through the proper channels
options: Chapter 5.
Chapter 7.
Chapter 11.
Chapter 15.
questions.explanation: Chapter 11 covers Petitions and Appeals, allowing aggrieved officers to seek redress.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Accountability** in service delivery is demonstrated when civil servants are required to:
options: Remain anonymous.
Be answerable for their actions and decisions.
Engage in political campaigns.
Avoid transparency.
questions.explanation: Accountability ensures civil servants are answerable for their official actions and decisions.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Federal Character Principle** (**Section 14**(3) of the Constitution) supports service delivery excellence by ensuring:
options: Appointments favor only one region.
Equitable representation and diversity, enhancing national cohesion in public service.
The civil service is governed by foreign nationals.
Only senior officers are trained.
questions.explanation: The Federal Character Principle ensures equitable representation of states and ethnic groups in appointments Reference: Section 14(3).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body must an officer appeal to if they are dissatisfied with a promotion exercise (**PSR** practice)
options: The Ministerial Tenders Board through procurement appeal procedures.
The Federal Civil Service Commission through direct petition alone.
The Inspector General of Police through administrative liaison.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) for allowances/petitions or the FCSC for promotion decisions.
questions.explanation: Officers dissatisfied with promotion exercises have a right of appeal to the FCSC (Rule 020805).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the consequence if an appeal/petition is illegible or meaningless (**Rule 110208(iii**))
options: It is automatically transferred to court.
It is returned for correction.
It will not be entertained.
The officer is given a warning.
questions.explanation: An appeal/petition will not be entertained if it is illegible or meaningless Reference: Rule 110208(iii)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Efficiency** in service delivery relies heavily on the proper implementation of:
options: Staff recruitment based on regional bias.
Performance management and continuous training.
Strict adherence to secrecy.
Mandatory delay of all processes.
questions.explanation: Efficiency relies on robust performance management and continuous training to ensure staff competence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **Civil Service Values** that underpin service delivery include Integrity, Neutrality, Service, Discipline, and:
options: Partisanship.
Secrecy.
Impunity.
Accountability.
questions.explanation: Core values include integrity, neutrality, service, discipline, and accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** ensures service continuity by applying the rules of the service to Federal Government **Parastatals** where:
options: The Parastatal has no internal rules or where internal rules have gaps/inconsistencies.
The Parastatal is fully funded but its internal regulations remain fully applicable.
The supervising Ministry issues temporary directives that do not override extant rules.
The agency operates as a research institution with complete internally approved rules.
questions.explanation: PSR applies where internal rules and procedures in Parastatals are nonexistent or inconsistent with general PSR principles (Rule 170104).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The establishment of appropriate staff committees (JSC, SSC) in Federal Government Parastatals is mandated to handle all matters of:
options: Budgetary allocation only.
Appointments, promotion, and discipline of officers.
Contract award and procurement.
External debt management.
questions.explanation: Staff committees in Parastatals handle appointments, promotion, and discipline of officers.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule covers the prohibition on seeking influence of prominent persons to gain appointment, transfer, promotion, or postings
questions.explanation: Rule 100427 prohibits seeking influence of prominent persons for career advantages.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 020606** states that officers may seek redress for grievance regarding posting instructions, provided they submit the complaint within:
options: Within one working day of receipt of the posting instruction.
Three working days of the posting instruction being released.
Within five working days after reporting at the new duty post.
Within thirty calendar days of the posting communication.
questions.explanation: Complaints/grievances must be submitted through appropriate channel within three working days Reference: Rule 020606.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that an officer on promotion who is dissatisfied with the outcome must exercise their right of appeal through:
options: Public communication channels to seek external pressure before review.
The appropriate channel and prescribed internal appeal procedures.
Direct court filing before exhausting internal administrative remedies.
Legislative petition to oversight committees outside prescribed procedure.
questions.explanation: Appeals must be exercised through the appropriate channel in accordance with Chapter 11 (Petitions and Appeals).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The principle of **Teamwork** in the Civil Service Handbook is emphasized to:
options: Create unnecessary competition.
Enhance collective efficiency and service delivery.
Encourage individual isolation.
Reduce staff accountability.
questions.explanation: Teamwork enhances collective efficiency and service delivery.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule ensures that officers who resigned are liable to refund the full cost of any training given, if governed by the bond
questions.explanation: An Officer who resigns will be liable to refund to the Government the full sum of money which he may be owing to Government or which, under the provisions of other rules or agreements, is refundable to Government (Rule 020904(a)(ii)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The mandatory requirement for **record-keeping** (CSH) primarily ensures:
options: Promotion of secrecy.
Accountability, transparency, and continuity in civil service operations.
Simplification of procurement thresholds.
Transfer of responsibility to junior officers.
questions.explanation: Proper record-keeping ensures accountability, transparency, and continuity in civil service operations.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the **Sabbatical Appointment** (**Rule 020413**) for senior officers
options: To grant an indefinite holiday.
The purpose of research and professional development of the Officer.
To negotiate new political contracts.
To avoid disciplinary action.
questions.explanation: Sabbatical Appointment is for the purpose of research and professional development of the Officer Reference: Rule 020413).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule covers the conditions for officers who leave the service other than by resignation or dismissal being eligible for proportionate leave
questions.explanation: Rule 020905 covers eligibility for proportionate leave for officers leaving the Service other than by resignation or dismissal.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** requires that appeals against promotion decisions must be considered with reference to the guidelines set out by the:
options: Ministry of Budget and Planning.
Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
National Assembly.
Trade Union Constitution.
questions.explanation: The consideration governing all promotions shall be in accordance with the guidelines set out by the Federal Civil Service Commission.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** Chapter applies to officers going on religious pilgrimages
options: Chapter 5.
Chapter 11.
Chapter 12.
Chapter 15.
questions.explanation: Chapter 12 covers Leave, Absence & Holidays, including leave for religious purposes (Rule 120232).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The Civil Service Handbook emphasizes that a civil servant's ultimate duty is to ensure:
options: Personal profit.
Service delivery is impartial and effective.
Political loyalty is paramount.
Records are kept secret indefinitely.
questions.explanation: The ultimate duty of civil servants is to provide impartial and effective service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: **PSR** **Rule 120233** allows for special leave with full pay for a period up to two weeks on **compassionate grounds** for:
options: Attendance at personal social ceremonies outside bereavement cases.
Burying a spouse, child, parent/parent of spouse.
Extended vacation to settle private family property matters.
Participation in partisan political conventions and campaigns.
questions.explanation: Compassionate leave (full pay) is granted for up to two weeks for the burial of spouse/child/parent/parent of spouse Reference: Rule 120233.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **FCSC** facilitates improvement in the welfare of employees and enriching the Administrative Procedures/Guidelines in the Public Service, serving as:
options: A political organ.
A mechanism for service professionalization.
A procurement body.
An external auditor.
questions.explanation: The FCSC promotes the professionalization of the Service and facilitates welfare improvement.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Service Delivery & Grievance, which action best demonstrates service delivery & grievance governance
options: Apply approved service delivery & grievance procedures and maintain complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
questions.explanation: Apply approved service delivery & grievance procedures and maintain complete records. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in service delivery & grievance Reference: Service Delivery & Grievance - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective service delivery & grievance, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service delivery & grievance compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in service delivery & grievance Reference: Service Delivery & Grievance - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on service delivery & grievance risk management within Service Delivery & Grievance
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in service delivery & grievance Reference: Service Delivery & Grievance - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling service delivery & grievance, which choice reflects proper administrative ethics standards
options: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in service delivery & grievance Reference: Service Delivery & Grievance - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain grievance handling in Service Delivery & Grievance
options: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in service delivery & grievance Reference: Service Delivery & Grievance - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Service Delivery & Grievance, which action best demonstrates performance management
options: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in service delivery & grievance Reference: Service Delivery & Grievance - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective service delivery & grievance, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure discipline and conduct
options: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
Bypass review and approval controls to save time.
questions.explanation: Address misconduct consistently under approved policy. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in service delivery & grievance Reference: Service Delivery & Grievance - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on anti-corruption safeguards within Service Delivery & Grievance
options: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in service delivery & grievance Reference: Service Delivery & Grievance - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling service delivery & grievance, which choice reflects proper documented procedure standards
options: Follow documented procedure and keep complete records.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.

Any activity requested by a government official.

questions.question: which option correctly addresses: Are Foreign Service Officers allowed to accept gifts or presentations for services rendered?
options: No, they are not allowed to accept gifts or presentations from any person. 14
Yes, if declared to the Ministry.
Only from diplomatic counterparts. □ 512
Yes, if the value is minimal.

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the chief instruction regarding the use of banking facilities for public funds?
options: Cash should be held for at least a week before banking. Hl.
The fullest possible use must be made of banking facilities, and all cash in excess of daily requirements must be paid into the bank account immediately.
All cash must be kept in the office safe.
Banking facilities are only for large transactions.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The fullest possible use must be made of banking facilities, and all cash in excess of daily requirements must be paid into the bank account immediately.). Financial Regulation 1101 states, "The fullest possible use must be made of banking facilities and all cash in excess of daily requirements must be paid into the bank account immediately."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the statement that correctly defines a key duty of cash advance holders regarding the use of funds?
options: They can delegate their responsibilities without supervision.
They can use other public funds for imprest purposes if needed.
They are only in charge for cash, not vouchers.a.
They are in charge for the correctness of vouchers signed by them.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They are in charge for the correctness of vouchers signed by them.). Financial Regulation 1008 states: "imprest holders must duly observe all regulations regarding the control of expenditure and the disbursement of public money and are in charge for the correctness of vouchers signed by them (see Regulation 410). They are also in charge for ensuring the early submission of paid vouchers for reimbursement. The imprest may be used only for the aim for which it is issued and imprest holders must not use other public funds for imprest purposes."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, choose the best answer for: If an officer delegates or entrusts the performance of duties to a subordinate officer, is the delegating officer relieved of accountability?
options: Yes, partially.
Only if the delegation is in writing. □
No, never.
Yes, completely.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (No, never.). Financial Regulation 125 states, "No officer must be relieved of his accountability should he delegate or entrust the performance of duties or any part thereof to his subordinate officer."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of the quota reserved for recruitment of persons with disabilities?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (5%). Rule 020202(b) reserves five percent (5%) of all recruitments for persons with disabilities.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of a key duty of the Accountant-General of the Federation regarding accounting systems and controls?
options: To formulate national economic policy. Q
To audit all public accounts annually.
To personally manage all financial transactions.
To provide adequate accounting systems and controls in all government arms.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To provide adequate accounting systems and controls in all government arms.). Financial Regulation 106 states the Accountant-General "has the accountability for providing adequate accounting systems and controls in the ministries, extra-ministerial offices, and other arms of Government."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: which option best describes the rule concerning the collection of revenue by a Sub-Accounting Officer?
options: They can delegate this duty to a junior staff member.
They are not involved in revenue collection. □
They are only accountable for revenue received in cash.
They are in charge for departmental control and collection.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (They are in charge for departmental control and collection.). Financial Regulation 217 implies a departmental control over revenue collection, and the Sub-Accounting Officer plays a key role in this.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, choose the best answer for: What happens to arrears of revenue still outstanding from previous years?
options: They must be included in the current year's arrears of revenue returns.
They are reported to the Minister of Finance.
They are automatically written off.
They are the duty of the previous Accounting Officer. Q 167
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (They must be included in the current year's arrears of revenue returns.). Financial Regulation 230(iii) states that arrears of revenue returns must include all arrears still outstanding from previous years if not written off.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: Can an officer to whom expenditure control has been delegated sub-delegate such duty?
options: Only for minor expenses. □
Yes, freely.
Yes, with the approval of the Accounting Officer.
No, never.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (No, never.). Financial Regulation 404 states: "He must not sub-delegate such duty wholly or partly, without the knowledge and approval of the officer controlling the vote."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, select the statement that correctly defines the duty of officers controlling votes regarding payment for services rendered?
options: To transfer all outstanding claims to a suspense account
To defer payments to the next financial year for budgetary reasons.
To secure payments are settled within the financial year in which they were rendered.
To only payment for services after all claims have been perfectly validated.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To secure payments are settled within the financial year in which they were rendered.). Financial Regulation 412(i) states: "Officers controlling votes are in charge for' ensuring that, as far as possible, disbursement for services rendered are settled within the financial year in which they were rendered."
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of the duty of Sub-Accounting Officers regarding receipt and licence books?
options: To print their own. □.
To dispose of them after use.
To leave them unsecured.
To secure their safe custody and proper use.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To secure their safe custody and proper use.). Financial Regulation 1130 states, "Sub-Accounting Officers must be in charge for the safe custody and proper use of all receipt and licence books issued to them." 239 Financial Regulations: Receipt and Licence Books

Centralized control.
Informal control. Q
External control.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Internal control.). Financial Regulation 1603(g) specifically highlights an "adequate system of Internal control".

questions.question: According to established rules, which role primarily bears the accountability for any discrepancies in financial records within a self-accounting unit?
options: The external auditor
The Accountant-General.
The junior staff.
The Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Accounting Officer.). The Accounting Officer is charged with ensuring strict observance of regulations and proper accounting, implying ultimate duty for discrepancies.

questions.question: Within government administration, which option aligns with the meaning of the implication of the Board of Survey's findings for an officer-in-charge?
options: The findings have no consequences for the officer.
The officer is solely accountable for any discrepancies.
The officer is immediately dismissed if a shortage is found.
The officer's accountability is discharged only after a satisfactory report is submitted.3
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The officer is solely accountable for any discrepancies.). The officer-in-charge must be present during the count and the Board's report is submitted for investigation of discrepancies, which implies the officer is in charge for the items under their charge. Part 3: Financial Regulations book: Pension Scheme in the Federal Public Service

questions.question: In official practice, choose the best answer for: The accountability for the Federal Budget is shared between which two arms of the Federal Government?
options: The Executive and the Judiciary
The Judiciary and the Legislature 379
The President and the Chief Justice Q
The Executive and the Legislature
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Executive and the Legislature). The accountability for the Federal Budget is shared between the Executive and the Legislature, which are two of the three arms of the Federal Government

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: What fiscal rule is observed under the Fiscal accountability Act 2007?
options: Total expenditure must not exceed total revenue.
Total revenue must always exceed total expenditure.
Total revenue is not a factor in determining total expenditure
Total expenditure can exceed total revenue if there are external grants.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Total expenditure must not exceed total revenue.). The fiscal rule being observed under the Fiscal duty Act 2007 is that total expenditure must not exceed total revenue

questions.question: In the public service context, select the statement that correctly defines a key accountability of the Chief Executive of a Parastatal to the Board?

To refuse to implement decisions that are not to his liking.
To implement the decisions and policies of the Board.
To manage the Board's personal affairs. □..
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To implement the decisions and policies of the Board.). The Chief Executive is expected to implement the decisions and policies of the Board.

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: The Manpower Development Office (MDO) has the accountability for coordinating what?
options: Service-wide administration of training and development.
The pool system for senior officers.
Preparation of draft Council memoranda on HRM matters.
The deployment of staff.a.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Service-wide administration of training and development.). The MDO has the accountability for ensuring the Service has its fair share of quality manpower and in this context, the Office coordinates Service-wide administration of training and development. 420 Section C Pat 1: Management of Human Resources in the Civil Service

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: If the chairman and other members cannot agree on the aim of a meeting, what is the chairman's accountability?
options: To move on with the meeting anyway.
To revert to the convening authority to seek clarification.
To dismiss the meeting entirely.
To force his own interpretation of the role on the members. Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To revert to the convening authority to seek clarification.). Where the chairman and the other members cannot achieve such an understanding or agreement on what the aim is, it is the chairman's duty to revert to the convening authority to seek and secure clarification.

To take a long vacation.
To blame others for the failure of the meeting.3.
To guarantee that the minutes are written and circulated, and that all follow-up actions are completed88.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To guarantee that the minutes are written and circulated, and that all follow-up actions are completed88.). It is the secretary's duty to secure that the minutes are written and circulated, and that all follow-up actions are completed.

They will have their own salary delayed
They will receive a formal warning.
They will be transferred to another department.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (They will be liable to surcharge and discipline for negligence.). According to Financial Regulation 1508, an officer who fails to promptly pass on information to the Director, Finance and Accounts Department is liable to surcharge and discipline for negligence and dereliction of duty.

options: A folder.
A file.
A memorandum. □.
A note.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (A file.). A file is a collection of official correspondence and other documents on a particular subject matter.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of the role of a "register of correspondences"?
options: To log all official meetings.
To document all financial transactions. □.
To list all the staff in a Ministry.
To list all the correspondences received and sent out by a Ministry.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To list all the correspondences received and sent out by a Ministry.). The register of correspondences is a list of all the correspondences received and sent out by a Ministry.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which time frame should an officer draft a reply to an item of correspondence?
options: Never, as it is the Secretariat's job. □.
Only for complex issues.
Where an item of correspondence demand a reply.
Only when specifically instructed.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Where an item of correspondence demand a reply.). Where an item of correspondence demand a reply, it is the duty of the Schedule Officer to whom it has been allocated to prepare a draft reply.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: What should letters always have?
options: A humorous opening.
A list of all previous correspondence.Q.
A personal salutation.
A heading briefly descriptive of the subject matter, a reference number, and a date.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (A heading briefly descriptive of the subject matter, a reference number, and a date.). Letters should always have a heading briefly descriptive of the subject matter and must bear a reference number and date.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option aligns with the meaning of the objective of an "open registry"?
options: To keep all personal files.
To keep all non-classified documents and correspondences.
To keep all classified documents.
To keep a log of all secret meetings. □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To keep all non-classified documents and correspondences.). The open registry is where non-classified documents and correspondences are kept and processed.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the public service context, which option best describes the aim of a "secret registry"?
options: To keep a document of all open meetings.a.
To keep all personal files.
To keep all non-classified documents.
To keep all classified documents and correspondences.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To keep all classified documents and correspondences.). The secret registry is where classified documents are handled.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: select the option that best answers: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: What does "Schemes of Service" refer to?
options: Retirement benefit plans.
Training programs for new recruits.
Disciplinary procedures

A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.). (A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.). Rule 010106 defines "Schemes of Service" as "a reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements, and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres."
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: choose the best answer for: How does the Attendance Register distinguish between staff who arrive on time and those who are late?
options: There is no distinction; everyone signs in the same place.
A supervisor makes a note next to the latecomer's name. Q.
Latecomers have to sign on a different sheet. 434
A red line is drawn across the register, and latecomers sign below it.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (A red line is drawn across the register, and latecomers sign below it.). Not later than 8.00 a.m., a red line should be drawn across the register each day so that staff who come late have to sign below the red line
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: A minute should contain a summary of the facts of the case, as well as what?
options: The officer's recommendations and conclusions.
A detailed financial analysis.a.
A list of all staff involved.
The officer's personal opinion.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (The officer's recommendations and conclusions.). A minute should contain a summary of the facts of the case, the officer's recommendations, and his conclusions.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the option that a key characteristic of a good minute?
options: It should be written in a conversational tone.
It should be verbose and include all personal thoughts.
It should only be written by senior officers.a.
It should be brief, factual, and free from grammatical errors.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (It should be brief, factual, and free from grammatical errors.). A good minute should be brief, factual, well-reasoned, and free from grammatical errors, spelling mistakes, and ambiguity.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which role should receive a copy of a handing-over note?
options: The officer's friends and family. 450
The officer's predecessor.
The public.Q.
The Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.). A copy of the handing-over note is to be sent to the Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: The handbook mentions that all files should be classified into what?
options: Secret and Open.
Urgent and Normal.a.
Temporary and Permanent.
Subject and Personal.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Subject and Personal.). All files should be classified into subject files and personal files.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: The procedure of creating a new file should be guided by what?
options: The opinion of the Head of Department. Q.
The number of documents.
The existing File Index.
The needs of the officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The existing File Index.). New files should be created based on the existing File Index to secure the consistency and logic of the file numbering system.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

options: It should only be written by senior officers. □.
It should be verbose and include all personal thoughts.
It should be brief, factual, and free from grammatical errors.
It should be written in a conversational tone.

questions.question: Within government administration, select the option that best answers: Why is it important for a chairman to prepare adequately for a meeting?
options: To show off his intelligence to the members.
To give the Secretariat less work to do.3.
To waste time before the meeting starts.
To secure a smooth and effective control of the meeting and earn the respect of the members..
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To secure a smooth and effective control of the meeting and earn the respect of the members..). It is only by preparing adequately that the chairman can assure himself of an effective control of the meeting and earn the full respect of the members.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: which option correctly addresses: A secretary should send out the notice of meeting and agenda to all members at least how many days before the meeting?
options: One day.
Two days.
Seven days. 474 Q.
Five working days.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Five working days.). The notice of meeting and agenda should be sent out to all members at least five working days before the meeting.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official practice, which option correctly addresses: A good minute should be what?
options: A list of all the jokes told at the meeting.a.
As verbose as possible.
A reflection of the secretary's personal opinions.
A faithful and accurate summary of what transpired at the meeting.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (A faithful and accurate summary of what transpired at the meeting.). A good minute should be a faithful and accurate summary of what transpired at the meeting.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: which office should sign a handing-over note?
options: The Head of Department.3.
Only the officer taking over.
Both the officer handing over and the officer taking over.
Only the officer handing over.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Both the officer handing over and the officer taking over.). The original and the two copies of the notes should be signed by both the Officer Handing Over and the Officer Taking Over.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Within government administration, choose the best answer for: A secretary should send out the notice of meeting and agenda to all members at least how many days before the meeting?
options: One day.
Seven days. □. 481
Five working days.
Two days.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Five working days.). The notice of meeting and agenda should be sent out to all members at least five working days before the meeting.

questions.question: which option correctly addresses: What should a Schedule Officer have, regarding their writing?
options: A good style.
A very informal style.
A complex style.
A poetic style. Q
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (A good style.). A Schedule Officer should have a good style.
